=== Akama Shrine (Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi Prefecture) Festival === Following the {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦の戦い}} in 1186 (Bunji 2), {{ill|Emperor Go-Toba|ja|後鳥羽天皇}} issued a decree to Nagato Province for the enshrinement of Emperor Antoku's remains at Amidaji Temple (now Akama Shrine). A commemorative festival called the "Senteikai" was held annually on his death anniversary (March 24), which later became the "Main Festival" on April 23 under the Gregorian calendar and was eventually moved to the current date of May 2.
On May 2, ceremonies such as the "Goryōmae Festival," attended by the {{ill|National Heike Association|ja|全国平家会}}, are held. On May 3, the "Nakajima Group's Pilgrimage Ceremony" follows, inspired by a story of loyalists visiting the shrine. This is succeeded by the "Upper-class Women's Procession" (Jōrō Dōchū), a tradition rooted in the courtesans and later geisha who honored Emperor Antoku. The procession features participants dressed in court attire, stepping in the traditional {{ill|Soto Hachimonji|ja|外八文字}} style through Shimonoseki.
The main festival concludes on May 4 with the {{ill|Shinko Festival|ja|神幸祭}}.
This festival, alongside the {{ill|Kokura Gion Festival|ja|小倉祇園祭}} across the Kanmon Straits in Kitakyushu, is known for the saying, "If it doesn't rain at Senteisai in Seki or Gion in Kokura, money will pour like rain," highlighting the lively crowds.
The festival is designated an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|無形民俗文化財}} of Shimonoseki City<ref>[http://www.stca-kanko.or.jp/sightseeing/city_summary/events/ Shimonoseki Tourism and Convention Association]</ref>.
== References == * 'Shinto Encyclopedia' (Heibonsha, reprinted by Rinsen Shoten, 1969), ISBN 4-653-01347-0.

revision.text:
[[File:下関豊前田商店街_先帝祭_上臈道中_壱.jpg|thumb| The Late Emperor's Festival: Procession of Ladies (Shimonoseki Toyomaeda Shopping Arcade)]] [[File:下関豊前田商店街_先帝祭_上臈道中_五.jpg|thumb| The Late Emperor's Festival: Procession of Ladies (Shimonoseki Toyomaeda Shopping Arcade)]] {{User:Immanuelle/Japanese Imperial Rituals updated}} '''Senteisai''' (先帝祭) is a festival commemorating the previous emperor.
== Classifications == Examples include: # {{ill|Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace|ja|宮中祭祀}} honoring the previous {{ill|Emperor of Japan|ja|天皇}}. These major festivals are held annually on the day of the late emperor's passing. # A festival held at {{ill|Akama Shrine|ja|赤間神宮}} in {{ill|Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi|ja|下関市}}, Yamaguchi Prefecture, from May 2 to May 4. In this case, the "previous emperor" specifically refers to {{ill|Emperor Antoku|ja|安徳天皇}}.
=== Imperial Court Rites === As one of the major rites defined by the {{ill|Imperial Household Rites Ordinance|ja|皇室祭祀令}}, it is held annually on the day of the previous emperor's passing. The actual name of the ritual replaces "Senteisai" with the posthumous title of the emperor.
Although the Imperial Household Rites Ordinance was abolished in 1947, the tradition continues as an Imperial Court rite. During the {{ill|Heisei period|ja|平成}}, the {{ill|Emperor Shōwa Festival|ja|昭和天皇祭}} was conducted, and following the abdication of Emperor Akihito on April 30, 2019, the Emperor Shōwa Festival continues as the most recent commemoration of a deceased emperor into the {{ill|Reiwa period|ja|令和}}.
On the day of Senteisai, it was previously designated a public holiday under the former laws "Holiday Law" and "Regulations on Holidays." However, it was removed from the list of holidays with the enactment of the current "Act on National Holidays" in 1948.

A commemorative festival called the "Senteikai" was held annually on his death anniversary (March 24), which later became the "Main Festival" on April 23 under the Gregorian calendar and was eventually moved to the current date of May 2.
On May 2, ceremonies such as the "Goryōmae Festival," attended by the {{ill|National Heike Association|ja|全国平家会}}, are held. On May 3, the "Nakajima Group's Pilgrimage Ceremony" follows, inspired by a story of loyalists visiting the shrine. This is succeeded by the "Upper-class Women's Procession" (Jōrō Dōchū), a tradition rooted in the courtesans and later geisha who honored Emperor Antoku. The procession features participants dressed in court attire, stepping in the traditional {{ill|Soto Hachimonji|ja|外八文字}} style through Shimonoseki.
The main festival concludes on May 4 with the {{ill|Shinko Festival|ja|神幸祭}}.
This festival, alongside the {{ill|Kokura Gion Festival|ja|小倉祇園祭}} across the Kanmon Straits in Kitakyushu, is known for the saying, "If it doesn't rain at Senteisai in Seki or Gion in Kokura, money will pour like rain," highlighting the lively crowds.
The festival is designated an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|無形民俗文化財}} of Shimonoseki City<ref>[http://www.stca-kanko.or.jp/sightseeing/city_summary/events/ Shimonoseki Tourism and Convention Association]</ref>.

=== Akama Shrine (Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi Prefecture) Festival === Following the {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦の戦い}} in 1186 (Bunji 2), {{ill|Emperor Go-Toba|ja|後鳥羽天皇}} issued a decree to Nagato Province for the enshrinement of Emperor Antoku's remains at Amidaji Temple (now Akama Shrine). A commemorative festival called the "Senteikai" was held annually on his death anniversary (March 24), which later became the "Main Festival" on April 23 under the Gregorian calendar and was eventually moved to the current date of May 2.
On May 2, ceremonies such as the "Goryōmae Festival," attended by the {{ill|National Heike Association|ja|全国平家会}}, are held. On May 3, the "Nakajima Group's Pilgrimage Ceremony" follows, inspired by a story of loyalists visiting the shrine. This is succeeded by the "Upper-class Women's Procession" (Jōrō Dōchū), a tradition rooted in the courtesans and later geisha who honored Emperor Antoku. The procession features participants dressed in court attire, stepping in the traditional {{ill|Soto Hachimonji|ja|外八文字}} style through Shimonoseki.
The main festival concludes on May 4 with the {{ill|Shinko Festival|ja|神幸祭}}.
This festival, alongside the {{ill|Kokura Gion Festival|ja|小倉祇園祭}} across the Kanmon Straits in Kitakyushu, is known for the saying, "If it doesn't rain at Senteisai in Seki or Gion in Kokura, money will pour like rain," highlighting the lively crowds.

During the {{ill|Heisei period|ja|平成}}, the {{ill|Emperor Shōwa Festival|ja|昭和天皇祭}} was conducted, and following the abdication of Emperor Akihito on April 30, 2019, the Emperor Shōwa Festival continues as the most recent commemoration of a deceased emperor into the {{ill|Reiwa period|ja|令和}}.
On the day of Senteisai, it was previously designated a public holiday under the former laws "Holiday Law" and "Regulations on Holidays." However, it was removed from the list of holidays with the enactment of the current "Act on National Holidays" in 1948.

=== Akama Shrine (Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi Prefecture) Festival === Following the {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦の戦い}} in 1186 (Bunji 2), {{ill|Emperor Go-Toba|ja|後鳥羽天皇}} issued a decree to Nagato Province for the enshrinement of Emperor Antoku's remains at Amidaji Temple (now Akama Shrine). A commemorative festival called the "Senteikai" was held annually on his death anniversary (March 24), which later became the "Main Festival" on April 23 under the Gregorian calendar and was eventually moved to the current date of May 2.
On May 2, ceremonies such as the "Goryōmae Festival," attended by the {{ill|National Heike Association|ja|全国平家会}}, are held. On May 3, the "Nakajima Group's Pilgrimage Ceremony" follows, inspired by a story of loyalists visiting the shrine. This is succeeded by the "Upper-class Women's Procession" (Jōrō Dōchū), a tradition rooted in the courtesans and later geisha who honored Emperor Antoku. The procession features participants dressed in court attire, stepping in the traditional {{ill|Soto Hachimonji|ja|外八文字}} style through Shimonoseki.
The main festival concludes on May 4 with the {{ill|Shinko Festival|ja|神幸祭}}.
This festival, alongside the {{ill|Kokura Gion Festival|ja|小倉祇園祭}} across the Kanmon Straits in Kitakyushu, is known for the saying, "If it doesn't rain at Senteisai in Seki or Gion in Kokura, money will pour like rain," highlighting the lively crowds.
The festival is designated an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|無形民俗文化財}} of Shimonoseki City<ref>[http://www.stca-kanko.or.jp/sightseeing/city_summary/events/ Shimonoseki Tourism and Convention Association]</ref>.

[[File:下関豊前田商店街_先帝祭_上臈道中_壱.jpg|thumb| The Late Emperor's Festival: Procession of Ladies (Shimonoseki Toyomaeda Shopping Arcade)]] [[File:下関豊前田商店街_先帝祭_上臈道中_五.jpg|thumb| The Late Emperor's Festival: Procession of Ladies (Shimonoseki Toyomaeda Shopping Arcade)]] {{User:Immanuelle/Japanese Imperial Rituals updated}} '''Senteisai''' (先帝祭) is a festival commemorating the previous emperor.
== Classifications == Examples include: # {{ill|Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace|ja|宮中祭祀}} honoring the previous {{ill|Emperor of Japan|ja|天皇}}. These major festivals are held annually on the day of the late emperor's passing. # A festival held at {{ill|Akama Shrine|ja|赤間神宮}} in {{ill|Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi|ja|下関市}}, Yamaguchi Prefecture, from May 2 to May 4. In this case, the "previous emperor" specifically refers to {{ill|Emperor Antoku|ja|安徳天皇}}.
=== Imperial Court Rites === As one of the major rites defined by the {{ill|Imperial Household Rites Ordinance|ja|皇室祭祀令}}, it is held annually on the day of the previous emperor's passing. The actual name of the ritual replaces "Senteisai" with the posthumous title of the emperor.
Although the Imperial Household Rites Ordinance was abolished in 1947, the tradition continues as an Imperial Court rite. During the {{ill|Heisei period|ja|平成}}, the {{ill|Emperor Shōwa Festival|ja|昭和天皇祭}} was conducted, and following the abdication of Emperor Akihito on April 30, 2019, the Emperor Shōwa Festival continues as the most recent commemoration of a deceased emperor into the {{ill|Reiwa period|ja|令和}}.
On the day of Senteisai, it was previously designated a public holiday under the former laws "Holiday Law" and "Regulations on Holidays." However, it was removed from the list of holidays with the enactment of the current "Act on National Holidays" in 1948.
{| class="wikitable" |+ Senteisai after the Meiji period |- ! Era !! Senteisai !! Date |- | {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} || {{ill|Emperor Kōmei|ja|孝明天皇}} Festival || January 30 |- | {{ill|Taishō|ja|大正}} || {{ill|Emperor Meiji|ja|明治天皇}} Festival || July 30 |- | {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} || {{ill|Emperor Taishō|ja|大正天皇}} Festival || December 25 |- | {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} || Emperor Shōwa Festival || January 7 |- | {{ill|Reiwa|ja|令和}} || Emperor Shōwa Festival || January 7 |}
=== Akama Shrine (Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi Prefecture) Festival === Following the {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦の戦い}} in 1186 (Bunji 2), {{ill|Emperor Go-Toba|ja|後鳥羽天皇}} issued a decree to Nagato Province for the enshrinement of Emperor Antoku's remains at Amidaji Temple (now Akama Shrine). A commemorative festival called the "Senteikai" was held annually on his death anniversary (March 24), which later became the "Main Festival" on April 23 under the Gregorian calendar and was eventually moved to the current date of May 2.
On May 2, ceremonies such as the "Goryōmae Festival," attended by the {{ill|National Heike Association|ja|全国平家会}}, are held. On May 3, the "Nakajima Group's Pilgrimage Ceremony" follows, inspired by a story of loyalists visiting the shrine. This is succeeded by the "Upper-class Women's Procession" (Jōrō Dōchū), a tradition rooted in the courtesans and later geisha who honored Emperor Antoku. The procession features participants dressed in court attire, stepping in the traditional {{ill|Soto Hachimonji|ja|外八文字}} style through Shimonoseki.
The main festival concludes on May 4 with the {{ill|Shinko Festival|ja|神幸祭}}.
This festival, alongside the {{ill|Kokura Gion Festival|ja|小倉祇園祭}} across the Kanmon Straits in Kitakyushu, is known for the saying, "If it doesn't rain at Senteisai in Seki or Gion in Kokura, money will pour like rain," highlighting the lively crowds.
The festival is designated an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|無形民俗文化財}} of Shimonoseki City<ref>[http://www.stca-kanko.or.jp/sightseeing/city_summary/events/ Shimonoseki Tourism and Convention Association]</ref>.

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Qq deprecated close]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
[[File:下関豊前田商店街_先帝祭_上臈道中_壱.jpg|thumb| The Late Emperor's Festival: Procession of Ladies (Shimonoseki Toyomaeda Shopping Arcade)]] [[File:下関豊前田商店街_先帝祭_上臈道中_五.jpg|thumb| The Late Emperor's Festival: Procession of Ladies (Shimonoseki Toyomaeda Shopping Arcade)]] {{User:Immanuelle/Japanese Imperial Rituals updated}} '''Senteisai''' (先帝祭) is a festival commemorating the previous emperor.
== Classifications == Examples include: # {{ill|Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace|ja|宮中祭祀}} honoring the previous {{ill|Emperor of Japan|ja|天皇}}. These major festivals are held annually on the day of the late emperor's passing. # A festival held at {{ill|Akama Shrine|ja|赤間神宮}} in {{ill|Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi|ja|下関市}}, Yamaguchi Prefecture, from May 2 to May 4. In this case, the "previous emperor" specifically refers to {{ill|Emperor Antoku|ja|安徳天皇}}.
=== Imperial Court Rites === As one of the major rites defined by the {{ill|Imperial Household Rites Ordinance|ja|皇室祭祀令}}, it is held annually on the day of the previous emperor's passing. The actual name of the ritual replaces "Senteisai" with the posthumous title of the emperor.
Although the Imperial Household Rites Ordinance was abolished in 1947, the tradition continues as an Imperial Court rite. During the {{ill|Heisei period|ja|平成}}, the {{ill|Emperor Shōwa Festival|ja|昭和天皇祭}} was conducted, and following the abdication of Emperor Akihito on April 30, 2019, the Emperor Shōwa Festival continues as the most recent commemoration of a deceased emperor into the {{ill|Reiwa period|ja|令和}}.
On the day of Senteisai, it was previously designated a public holiday under the former laws "Holiday Law" and "Regulations on Holidays." However, it was removed from the list of holidays with the enactment of the current "Act on National Holidays" in 1948.
{| class="wikitable" |+ Senteisai after the Meiji period |- ! Era !! Senteisai !! Date |- | {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} || {{ill|Emperor Kōmei|ja|孝明天皇}} Festival || January 30 |- | {{ill|Taishō|ja|大正}} || {{ill|Emperor Meiji|ja|明治天皇}} Festival || July 30 |- | {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} || {{ill|Emperor Taishō|ja|大正天皇}} Festival || December 25 |- | {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} || Emperor Shōwa Festival || January 7 |- | {{ill|Reiwa|ja|令和}} || Emperor Shōwa Festival || January 7 |}
=== Akama Shrine (Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi Prefecture) Festival === Following the {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦の戦い}} in 1186 (Bunji 2), {{ill|Emperor Go-Toba|ja|後鳥羽天皇}} issued a decree to Nagato Province for the enshrinement of Emperor Antoku's remains at Amidaji Temple (now Akama Shrine). A commemorative festival called the "Senteikai" was held annually on his death anniversary (March 24), which later became the "Main Festival" on April 23 under the Gregorian calendar and was eventually moved to the current date of May 2.
On May 2, ceremonies such as the "Goryōmae Festival," attended by the {{ill|National Heike Association|ja|全国平家会}}, are held. On May 3, the "Nakajima Group's Pilgrimage Ceremony" follows, inspired by a story of loyalists visiting the shrine. This is succeeded by the "Upper-class Women's Procession" (Jōrō Dōchū), a tradition rooted in the courtesans and later geisha who honored Emperor Antoku. The procession features participants dressed in court attire, stepping in the traditional {{ill|Soto Hachimonji|ja|外八文字}} style through Shimonoseki.
The main festival concludes on May 4 with the {{ill|Shinko Festival|ja|神幸祭}}.
This festival, alongside the {{ill|Kokura Gion Festival|ja|小倉祇園祭}} across the Kanmon Straits in Kitakyushu, is known for the saying, "If it doesn't rain at Senteisai in Seki or Gion in Kokura, money will pour like rain," highlighting the lively crowds.
The festival is designated an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|無形民俗文化財}} of Shimonoseki City<ref>[http://www.stca-kanko.or.jp/sightseeing/city_summary/events/ Shimonoseki Tourism and Convention Association]</ref>.
== References == * 'Shinto Encyclopedia' (Heibonsha, reprinted by Rinsen Shoten, 1969), ISBN 4-653-01347-0.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Kokuki|ja|国忌}} * {{ill|Shimonoseki red-light district|ja|下関の遊廓}}
== External Links == * [http://www.tiki.ne.jp/~akama-jingu/senteisai.html Senteisai] (Akama Shrine Official Website)

The festival is designated an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|無形民俗文化財}} of Shimonoseki City<ref>[http://www.stca-kanko.or.jp/sightseeing/city_summary/events/ Shimonoseki Tourism and Convention Association]</ref>.

[[File:下関豊前田商店街_先帝祭_上臈道中_壱.jpg|thumb| The Late Emperor's Festival: Procession of Ladies (Shimonoseki Toyomaeda Shoqqing Arcade)]] [[File:下関豊前田商店街_先帝祭_上臈道中_五.jpg|thumb| The Late Emperor's Festival: Procession of Ladies (Shimonoseki Toyomaeda Shoqqing Arcade)]] {{User:Immanuelle/Japanese Imperial Rituals updated}} '''Senteisai''' (先帝祭) is a festival commemorating the previous emperor.
== Classifications == Examples include: # {{ill|Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace|ja|宮中祭祀}} honoring the previous {{ill|Emperor of Japan|ja|天皇}}. These major festivals are held annually on the day of the late emperor's passing. # A festival held at {{ill|Akama Shrine|ja|赤間神宮}} in {{ill|Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi|ja|下関市}}, Yamaguchi Prefecture, from May 2 to May 4. In this case, the "previous emperor" specifically refers to {{ill|Emperor Antoku|ja|安徳天皇}}.
=== Imperial Court Rites === As one of the major rites defined by the {{ill|Imperial Household Rites Ordinance|ja|皇室祭祀令}}, it is held annually on the day of the previous emperor's passing. The actual name of the ritual replaces "Senteisai" with the posthumous title of the emperor.
Although the Imperial Household Rites Ordinance was abolished in 1947, the tradition continues as an Imperial Court rite. During the {{ill|Heisei period|ja|平成}}, the {{ill|Emperor Shōwa Festival|ja|昭和天皇祭}} was conducted, and following the abdication of Emperor Akihito on April 30, 2019, the Emperor Shōwa Festival continues as the most recent commemoration of a deceased emperor into the {{ill|Reiwa period|ja|令和}}.
On the day of Senteisai, it was previously designated a public holiday under the former laws "Holiday Law" and "Regulations on Holidays." However, it was removed from the list of holidays with the enactment of the current "Act on National Holidays" in 1948.
{| class="wikitable" |+ Senteisai after the Meiji period |- ! Era !! Senteisai !! Date |- | {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} || {{ill|Emperor Kōmei|ja|孝明天皇}} Festival || January 30 |- | {{ill|Taishō|ja|大正}} || {{ill|Emperor Meiji|ja|明治天皇}} Festival || July 30 |- | {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} || {{ill|Emperor Taishō|ja|大正天皇}} Festival || December 25 |- | {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} || Emperor Shōwa Festival || January 7 |- | {{ill|Reiwa|ja|令和}} || Emperor Shōwa Festival || January 7 |}
=== Akama Shrine (Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi Prefecture) Festival === Following the {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦の戦い}} in 1186 (Bunji 2), {{ill|Emperor Go-Toba|ja|後鳥羽天皇}} issued a decree to Nagato Province for the enshrinement of Emperor Antoku's remains at Amidaji Temple (now Akama Shrine). A commemorative festival called the "Senteikai" was held annually on his death anniversary (March 24), which later became the "Main Festival" on April 23 under the Gregorian calendar and was eventually moved to the current date of May 2.
On May 2, ceremonies such as the "Goryōmae Festival," attended by the {{ill|National Heike Association|ja|全国平家会}}, are held. On May 3, the "Nakajima Group's Pilgrimage Ceremony" follows, inspired by a story of loyalists visiting the shrine. This is succeeded by the "Uqqer-class Women's Procession" (Jōrō Dōchū), a tradition rooted in the courtesans and later geisha who honored Emperor Antoku. The procession features participants dressed in court attire, steqqing in the traditional {{ill|Soto Hachimonji|ja|外八文字}} style through Shimonoseki.
The main festival concludes on May 4 with the {{ill|Shinko Festival|ja|神幸祭}}.
This festival, alongside the {{ill|Kokura Gion Festival|ja|小倉祇園祭}} across the Kanmon Straits in Kitakyushu, is known for the saying, "If it doesn't rain at Senteisai in Seki or Gion in Kokura, money will pour like rain," highlighting the lively crowds.
The festival is designated an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|無形民俗文化財}} of Shimonoseki City<ref>[http://www.stca-kanko.or.jp/sightseeing/city_summary/events/ Shimonoseki Tourism and Convention Association]</ref>.

revision.text:
[[File:下関豊前田商店街_先帝祭_上臈道中_壱.jpg|thumb| The Late Emperor's Festival: Procession of Ladies (Shimonoseki Toyomaeda Shoqqing Arcade)]] [[File:下関豊前田商店街_先帝祭_上臈道中_五.jpg|thumb| The Late Emperor's Festival: Procession of Ladies (Shimonoseki Toyomaeda Shoqqing Arcade)]] {{User:Immanuelle/Japanese Imperial Rituals updated}} '''Senteisai''' (先帝祭) is a festival commemorating the previous emperor.
== Classifications == Examples include: # {{ill|Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace|ja|宮中祭祀}} honoring the previous {{ill|Emperor of Japan|ja|天皇}}. These major festivals are held annually on the day of the late emperor's passing. # A festival held at {{ill|Akama Shrine|ja|赤間神宮}} in {{ill|Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi|ja|下関市}}, Yamaguchi Prefecture, from May 2 to May 4. In this case, the "previous emperor" specifically refers to {{ill|Emperor Antoku|ja|安徳天皇}}.
=== Imperial Court Rites === As one of the major rites defined by the {{ill|Imperial Household Rites Ordinance|ja|皇室祭祀令}}, it is held annually on the day of the previous emperor's passing. The actual name of the ritual replaces "Senteisai" with the posthumous title of the emperor.
Although the Imperial Household Rites Ordinance was abolished in 1947, the tradition continues as an Imperial Court rite. During the {{ill|Heisei period|ja|平成}}, the {{ill|Emperor Shōwa Festival|ja|昭和天皇祭}} was conducted, and following the abdication of Emperor Akihito on April 30, 2019, the Emperor Shōwa Festival continues as the most recent commemoration of a deceased emperor into the {{ill|Reiwa period|ja|令和}}.
On the day of Senteisai, it was previously designated a public holiday under the former laws "Holiday Law" and "Regulations on Holidays." However, it was removed from the list of holidays with the enactment of the current "Act on National Holidays" in 1948.
{| class="wikitable" |+ Senteisai after the Meiji period |- ! Era !! Senteisai !! Date |- | {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} || {{ill|Emperor Kōmei|ja|孝明天皇}} Festival || January 30 |- | {{ill|Taishō|ja|大正}} || {{ill|Emperor Meiji|ja|明治天皇}} Festival || July 30 |- | {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} || {{ill|Emperor Taishō|ja|大正天皇}} Festival || December 25 |- | {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} || Emperor Shōwa Festival || January 7 |- | {{ill|Reiwa|ja|令和}} || Emperor Shōwa Festival || January 7 |}
=== Akama Shrine (Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi Prefecture) Festival === Following the {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦の戦い}} in 1186 (Bunji 2), {{ill|Emperor Go-Toba|ja|後鳥羽天皇}} issued a decree to Nagato Province for the enshrinement of Emperor Antoku's remains at Amidaji Temple (now Akama Shrine). A commemorative festival called the "Senteikai" was held annually on his death anniversary (March 24), which later became the "Main Festival" on April 23 under the Gregorian calendar and was eventually moved to the current date of May 2.
On May 2, ceremonies such as the "Goryōmae Festival," attended by the {{ill|National Heike Association|ja|全国平家会}}, are held. On May 3, the "Nakajima Group's Pilgrimage Ceremony" follows, inspired by a story of loyalists visiting the shrine. This is succeeded by the "Uqqer-class Women's Procession" (Jōrō Dōchū), a tradition rooted in the courtesans and later geisha who honored Emperor Antoku. The procession features participants dressed in court attire, steqqing in the traditional {{ill|Soto Hachimonji|ja|外八文字}} style through Shimonoseki.
The main festival concludes on May 4 with the {{ill|Shinko Festival|ja|神幸祭}}.
This festival, alongside the {{ill|Kokura Gion Festival|ja|小倉祇園祭}} across the Kanmon Straits in Kitakyushu, is known for the saying, "If it doesn't rain at Senteisai in Seki or Gion in Kokura, money will pour like rain," highlighting the lively crowds.
The festival is designated an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|無形民俗文化財}} of Shimonoseki City<ref>[http://www.stca-kanko.or.jp/sightseeing/city_summary/events/ Shimonoseki Tourism and Convention Association]</ref>.
== References == * 'Shinto Encyclopedia' (Heibonsha, reprinted by Rinsen Shoten, 1969), ISBN 4-653-01347-0.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Kokuki|ja|国忌}} * {{ill|Shimonoseki red-light district|ja|下関の遊廓}}
== External Links == * [http://www.tiki.ne.jp/~akama-jingu/senteisai.html Senteisai] (Akama Shrine Official Website)

[[File:下関豊前田商店街_先帝祭_上臈道中_壱.jpg|thumb| The Late Emperor's Festival: Procession of Ladies (Shimonoseki Toyomaeda Shoqqing Arcade)]] [[File:下関豊前田商店街_先帝祭_上臈道中_五.jpg|thumb| The Late Emperor's Festival: Procession of Ladies (Shimonoseki Toyomaeda Shoqqing Arcade)]] {{User:Immanuelle/Japanese Imperial Rituals updated}} '''Senteisai''' (先帝祭) is a festival commemorating the previous emperor.
== Classifications == Examples include: # {{ill|Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace|ja|宮中祭祀}} honoring the previous {{ill|Emperor of Japan|ja|天皇}}. These major festivals are held annually on the day of the late emperor's passing. # A festival held at {{ill|Akama Shrine|ja|赤間神宮}} in {{ill|Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi|ja|下関市}}, Yamaguchi Prefecture, from May 2 to May 4. In this case, the "previous emperor" specifically refers to {{ill|Emperor Antoku|ja|安徳天皇}}.
=== Imperial Court Rites === As one of the major rites defined by the {{ill|Imperial Household Rites Ordinance|ja|皇室祭祀令}}, it is held annually on the day of the previous emperor's passing. The actual name of the ritual replaces "Senteisai" with the posthumous title of the emperor.
Although the Imperial Household Rites Ordinance was abolished in 1947, the tradition continues as an Imperial Court rite. During the {{ill|Heisei period|ja|平成}}, the {{ill|Emperor Shōwa Festival|ja|昭和天皇祭}} was conducted, and following the abdication of Emperor Akihito on April 30, 2019, the Emperor Shōwa Festival continues as the most recent commemoration of a deceased emperor into the {{ill|Reiwa period|ja|令和}}.
On the day of Senteisai, it was previously designated a public holiday under the former laws "Holiday Law" and "Regulations on Holidays." However, it was removed from the list of holidays with the enactment of the current "Act on National Holidays" in 1948.
{| class="wikitable" |+ Senteisai after the Meiji period |- ! Era !! Senteisai !! Date |- | {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} || {{ill|Emperor Kōmei|ja|孝明天皇}} Festival || January 30 |- | {{ill|Taishō|ja|大正}} || {{ill|Emperor Meiji|ja|明治天皇}} Festival || July 30 |- | {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} || {{ill|Emperor Taishō|ja|大正天皇}} Festival || December 25 |- | {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} || Emperor Shōwa Festival || January 7 |- | {{ill|Reiwa|ja|令和}} || Emperor Shōwa Festival || January 7 |}
=== Akama Shrine (Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi Prefecture) Festival === Following the {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦の戦い}} in 1186 (Bunji 2), {{ill|Emperor Go-Toba|ja|後鳥羽天皇}} issued a decree to Nagato Province for the enshrinement of Emperor Antoku's remains at Amidaji Temple (now Akama Shrine). A commemorative festival called the "Senteikai" was held annually on his death anniversary (March 24), which later became the "Main Festival" on April 23 under the Gregorian calendar and was eventually moved to the current date of May 2.
On May 2, ceremonies such as the "Goryōmae Festival," attended by the {{ill|National Heike Association|ja|全国平家会}}, are held. On May 3, the "Nakajima Group's Pilgrimage Ceremony" follows, inspired by a story of loyalists visiting the shrine. This is succeeded by the "Uqqer-class Women's Procession" (Jōrō Dōchū), a tradition rooted in the courtesans and later geisha who honored Emperor Antoku. The procession features participants dressed in court attire, steqqing in the traditional {{ill|Soto Hachimonji|ja|外八文字}} style through Shimonoseki.
The main festival concludes on May 4 with the {{ill|Shinko Festival|ja|神幸祭}}.
This festival, alongside the {{ill|Kokura Gion Festival|ja|小倉祇園祭}} across the Kanmon Straits in Kitakyushu, is known for the saying, "If it doesn't rain at Senteisai in Seki or Gion in Kokura, money will pour like rain," highlighting the lively crowds.
The festival is designated an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|無形民俗文化財}} of Shimonoseki City<ref>[http://www.stca-kanko.or.jp/sightseeing/city_summary/events/ Shimonoseki Tourism and Convention Association]</ref>.
== References == * 'Shinto Encyclopedia' (Heibonsha, reprinted by Rinsen Shoten, 1969), ISBN 4-653-01347-0.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Kokuki|ja|国忌}} * {{ill|Shimonoseki red-light district|ja|下関の遊廓}}
== External Links == * [http://www.tiki.ne.jp/~akama-jingu/senteisai.html Senteisai] (Akama Shrine Official Website)

revision.comment: Moving from [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] to [[Category:Completion tier 1]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
[[File:下関豊前田商店街_先帝祭_上臈道中_壱.jpg|thumb| The Late Emperor's Festival: Procession of Ladies (Shimonoseki Toyomaeda Shoqqing Arcade)]] [[File:下関豊前田商店街_先帝祭_上臈道中_五.jpg|thumb| The Late Emperor's Festival: Procession of Ladies (Shimonoseki Toyomaeda Shoqqing Arcade)]] {{User:Immanuelle/Japanese Imperial Rituals updated}} '''Senteisai''' (先帝祭) is a festival commemorating the previous emperor.
== Classifications == Examples include: # {{ill|Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace|ja|宮中祭祀}} honoring the previous {{ill|Emperor of Japan|ja|天皇}}. These major festivals are held annually on the day of the late emperor's passing. # A festival held at {{ill|Akama Shrine|ja|赤間神宮}} in {{ill|Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi|ja|下関市}}, Yamaguchi Prefecture, from May 2 to May 4. In this case, the "previous emperor" specifically refers to {{ill|Emperor Antoku|ja|安徳天皇}}.
=== Imperial Court Rites === As one of the major rites defined by the {{ill|Imperial Household Rites Ordinance|ja|皇室祭祀令}}, it is held annually on the day of the previous emperor's passing. The actual name of the ritual replaces "Senteisai" with the posthumous title of the emperor.
Although the Imperial Household Rites Ordinance was abolished in 1947, the tradition continues as an Imperial Court rite. During the {{ill|Heisei period|ja|平成}}, the {{ill|Emperor Shōwa Festival|ja|昭和天皇祭}} was conducted, and following the abdication of Emperor Akihito on April 30, 2019, the Emperor Shōwa Festival continues as the most recent commemoration of a deceased emperor into the {{ill|Reiwa period|ja|令和}}.
On the day of Senteisai, it was previously designated a public holiday under the former laws "Holiday Law" and "Regulations on Holidays." However, it was removed from the list of holidays with the enactment of the current "Act on National Holidays" in 1948.
{| class="wikitable" |+ Senteisai after the Meiji period |- ! Era !! Senteisai !! Date |- | {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} || {{ill|Emperor Kōmei|ja|孝明天皇}} Festival || January 30 |- | {{ill|Taishō|ja|大正}} || {{ill|Emperor Meiji|ja|明治天皇}} Festival || July 30 |- | {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} || {{ill|Emperor Taishō|ja|大正天皇}} Festival || December 25 |- | {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} || Emperor Shōwa Festival || January 7 |- | {{ill|Reiwa|ja|令和}} || Emperor Shōwa Festival || January 7 |}
=== Akama Shrine (Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi Prefecture) Festival === Following the {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦の戦い}} in 1186 (Bunji 2), {{ill|Emperor Go-Toba|ja|後鳥羽天皇}} issued a decree to Nagato Province for the enshrinement of Emperor Antoku's remains at Amidaji Temple (now Akama Shrine). A commemorative festival called the "Senteikai" was held annually on his death anniversary (March 24), which later became the "Main Festival" on April 23 under the Gregorian calendar and was eventually moved to the current date of May 2.
On May 2, ceremonies such as the "Goryōmae Festival," attended by the {{ill|National Heike Association|ja|全国平家会}}, are held. On May 3, the "Nakajima Group's Pilgrimage Ceremony" follows, inspired by a story of loyalists visiting the shrine. This is succeeded by the "Uqqer-class Women's Procession" (Jōrō Dōchū), a tradition rooted in the courtesans and later geisha who honored Emperor Antoku. The procession features participants dressed in court attire, steqqing in the traditional {{ill|Soto Hachimonji|ja|外八文字}} style through Shimonoseki.
The main festival concludes on May 4 with the {{ill|Shinko Festival|ja|神幸祭}}.
This festival, alongside the {{ill|Kokura Gion Festival|ja|小倉祇園祭}} across the Kanmon Straits in Kitakyushu, is known for the saying, "If it doesn't rain at Senteisai in Seki or Gion in Kokura, money will pour like rain," highlighting the lively crowds.
The festival is designated an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|無形民俗文化財}} of Shimonoseki City<ref>[http://www.stca-kanko.or.jp/sightseeing/city_summary/events/ Shimonoseki Tourism and Convention Association]</ref>.
== References == * 'Shinto Encyclopedia' (Heibonsha, reprinted by Rinsen Shoten, 1969), ISBN 4-653-01347-0.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Kokuki|ja|国忌}} * {{ill|Shimonoseki red-light district|ja|下関の遊廓}}
== External Links == * [http://www.tiki.ne.jp/~akama-jingu/senteisai.html Senteisai] (Akama Shrine Official Website)

[[File:下関豊前田商店街_先帝祭_上臈道中_壱.jpg|thumb| The Late Emperor's Festival: Procession of Ladies (Shimonoseki Toyomaeda Shoqqing Arcade)]] [[File:下関豊前田商店街_先帝祭_上臈道中_五.jpg|thumb| The Late Emperor's Festival: Procession of Ladies (Shimonoseki Toyomaeda Shoqqing Arcade)]] {{User:Immanuelle/Japanese Imperial Rituals updated}} '''Senteisai''' (先帝祭) is a festival commemorating the previous emperor.
== Classifications == Examples include: # {{ill|Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace|ja|宮中祭祀}} honoring the previous {{ill|Emperor of Japan|ja|天皇}}. These major festivals are held annually on the day of the late emperor's passing. # A festival held at {{ill|Akama Shrine|ja|赤間神宮}} in {{ill|Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi|ja|下関市}}, Yamaguchi Prefecture, from May 2 to May 4. In this case, the "previous emperor" specifically refers to {{ill|Emperor Antoku|ja|安徳天皇}}.
=== Imperial Court Rites === As one of the major rites defined by the {{ill|Imperial Household Rites Ordinance|ja|皇室祭祀令}}, it is held annually on the day of the previous emperor's passing. The actual name of the ritual replaces "Senteisai" with the posthumous title of the emperor.
Although the Imperial Household Rites Ordinance was abolished in 1947, the tradition continues as an Imperial Court rite. During the {{ill|Heisei period|ja|平成}}, the {{ill|Emperor Shōwa Festival|ja|昭和天皇祭}} was conducted, and following the abdication of Emperor Akihito on April 30, 2019, the Emperor Shōwa Festival continues as the most recent commemoration of a deceased emperor into the {{ill|Reiwa period|ja|令和}}.
On the day of Senteisai, it was previously designated a public holiday under the former laws "Holiday Law" and "Regulations on Holidays." However, it was removed from the list of holidays with the enactment of the current "Act on National Holidays" in 1948.
{| class="wikitable" |+ Senteisai after the Meiji period |- ! Era !! Senteisai !! Date |- | {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} || {{ill|Emperor Kōmei|ja|孝明天皇}} Festival || January 30 |- | {{ill|Taishō|ja|大正}} || {{ill|Emperor Meiji|ja|明治天皇}} Festival || July 30 |- | {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} || {{ill|Emperor Taishō|ja|大正天皇}} Festival || December 25 |- | {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} || Emperor Shōwa Festival || January 7 |- | {{ill|Reiwa|ja|令和}} || Emperor Shōwa Festival || January 7 |}
=== Akama Shrine (Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi Prefecture) Festival === Following the {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦の戦い}} in 1186 (Bunji 2), {{ill|Emperor Go-Toba|ja|後鳥羽天皇}} issued a decree to Nagato Province for the enshrinement of Emperor Antoku's remains at Amidaji Temple (now Akama Shrine). A commemorative festival called the "Senteikai" was held annually on his death anniversary (March 24), which later became the "Main Festival" on April 23 under the Gregorian calendar and was eventually moved to the current date of May 2.
On May 2, ceremonies such as the "Goryōmae Festival," attended by the {{ill|National Heike Association|ja|全国平家会}}, are held. On May 3, the "Nakajima Group's Pilgrimage Ceremony" follows, inspired by a story of loyalists visiting the shrine. This is succeeded by the "Uqqer-class Women's Procession" (Jōrō Dōchū), a tradition rooted in the courtesans and later geisha who honored Emperor Antoku. The procession features participants dressed in court attire, steqqing in the traditional {{ill|Soto Hachimonji|ja|外八文字}} style through Shimonoseki.
The main festival concludes on May 4 with the {{ill|Shinko Festival|ja|神幸祭}}.
This festival, alongside the {{ill|Kokura Gion Festival|ja|小倉祇園祭}} across the Kanmon Straits in Kitakyushu, is known for the saying, "If it doesn't rain at Senteisai in Seki or Gion in Kokura, money will pour like rain," highlighting the lively crowds.
The festival is designated an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|無形民俗文化財}} of Shimonoseki City<ref>[http://www.stca-kanko.or.jp/sightseeing/city_summary/events/ Shimonoseki Tourism and Convention Association]</ref>.
== References == * 'Shinto Encyclopedia' (Heibonsha, reprinted by Rinsen Shoten, 1969), ISBN 4-653-01347-0.

[[File:下関豊前田商店街_先帝祭_上臈道中_壱.jpg|thumb| The Late Emperor's Festival: Procession of Ladies (Shimonoseki Toyomaeda Sho11ing Arcade)]] [[File:下関豊前田商店街_先帝祭_上臈道中_五.jpg|thumb| The Late Emperor's Festival: Procession of Ladies (Shimonoseki Toyomaeda Sho11ing Arcade)]] {{User:Immanuelle/Japanese Imperial Rituals updated}} '''Senteisai''' (先帝祭) is a festival commemorating the previous emperor.
== Classifications == Examples include: # {{ill|Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace|ja|宮中祭祀}} honoring the previous {{ill|Emperor of Japan|ja|天皇}}. These major festivals are held annually on the day of the late emperor's passing. # A festival held at {{ill|Akama Shrine|ja|赤間神宮}} in {{ill|Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi|ja|下関市}}, Yamaguchi Prefecture, from May 2 to May 4. In this case, the "previous emperor" specifically refers to {{ill|Emperor Antoku|ja|安徳天皇}}.
=== Imperial Court Rites === As one of the major rites defined by the {{ill|Imperial Household Rites Ordinance|ja|皇室祭祀令}}, it is held annually on the day of the previous emperor's passing. The actual name of the ritual replaces "Senteisai" with the posthumous title of the emperor.
Although the Imperial Household Rites Ordinance was abolished in 1947, the tradition continues as an Imperial Court rite. During the {{ill|Heisei period|ja|平成}}, the {{ill|Emperor Shōwa Festival|ja|昭和天皇祭}} was conducted, and following the abdication of Emperor Akihito on April 30, 2019, the Emperor Shōwa Festival continues as the most recent commemoration of a deceased emperor into the {{ill|Reiwa period|ja|令和}}.
On the day of Senteisai, it was previously designated a public holiday under the former laws "Holiday Law" and "Regulations on Holidays." However, it was removed from the list of holidays with the enactment of the current "Act on National Holidays" in 1948.
{| class="wikitable" |+ Senteisai after the Meiji period |- ! Era !! Senteisai !! Date |- | {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} || {{ill|Emperor Kōmei|ja|孝明天皇}} Festival || January 30 |- | {{ill|Taishō|ja|大正}} || {{ill|Emperor Meiji|ja|明治天皇}} Festival || July 30 |- | {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} || {{ill|Emperor Taishō|ja|大正天皇}} Festival || December 25 |- | {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} || Emperor Shōwa Festival || January 7 |- | {{ill|Reiwa|ja|令和}} || Emperor Shōwa Festival || January 7 |}
=== Akama Shrine (Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi Prefecture) Festival === Following the {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦の戦い}} in 1186 (Bunji 2), {{ill|Emperor Go-Toba|ja|後鳥羽天皇}} issued a decree to Nagato Province for the enshrinement of Emperor Antoku's remains at Amidaji Temple (now Akama Shrine). A commemorative festival called the "Senteikai" was held annually on his death anniversary (March 24), which later became the "Main Festival" on April 23 under the Gregorian calendar and was eventually moved to the current date of May 2.
On May 2, ceremonies such as the "Goryōmae Festival," attended by the {{ill|National Heike Association|ja|全国平家会}}, are held. On May 3, the "Nakajima Group's Pilgrimage Ceremony" follows, inspired by a story of loyalists visiting the shrine. This is succeeded by the "U11er-class Women's Procession" (Jōrō Dōchū), a tradition rooted in the courtesans and later geisha who honored Emperor Antoku. The procession features participants dressed in court attire, ste11ing in the traditional {{ill|Soto Hachimonji|ja|外八文字}} style through Shimonoseki.
The main festival concludes on May 4 with the {{ill|Shinko Festival|ja|神幸祭}}.
This festival, alongside the {{ill|Kokura Gion Festival|ja|小倉祇園祭}} across the Kanmon Straits in Kitakyushu, is known for the saying, "If it doesn't rain at Senteisai in Seki or Gion in Kokura, money will pour like rain," highlighting the lively crowds.
The festival is designated an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|無形民俗文化財}} of Shimonoseki City<ref>[http://www.stca-kanko.or.jp/sightseeing/city_summary/events/ Shimonoseki Tourism and Convention Association]</ref>.
== References == * 'Shinto Encyclopedia' (Heibonsha, reprinted by Rinsen Shoten, 1969), ISBN 4-653-01347-0.

[[File:下関豊前田商店街_先帝祭_上臈道中_壱.jpg|thumb| The Late Emperor's Festival: Procession of Ladies (Shimonoseki Toyomaeda Sho11ing Arcade)]] [[File:下関豊前田商店街_先帝祭_上臈道中_五.jpg|thumb| The Late Emperor's Festival: Procession of Ladies (Shimonoseki Toyomaeda Sho11ing Arcade)]] {{User:Immanuelle/Japanese Imperial Rituals updated}} '''Senteisai''' (先帝祭) is a festival commemorating the previous emperor.
== Classifications == Examples include: # {{ill|Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace|ja|宮中祭祀}} honoring the previous {{ill|Emperor of Japan|ja|天皇}}. These major festivals are held annually on the day of the late emperor's passing. # A festival held at {{ill|Akama Shrine|ja|赤間神宮}} in {{ill|Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi|ja|下関市}}, Yamaguchi Prefecture, from May 2 to May 4. In this case, the "previous emperor" specifically refers to {{ill|Emperor Antoku|ja|安徳天皇}}.
=== Imperial Court Rites === As one of the major rites defined by the {{ill|Imperial Household Rites Ordinance|ja|皇室祭祀令}}, it is held annually on the day of the previous emperor's passing. The actual name of the ritual replaces "Senteisai" with the posthumous title of the emperor.
Although the Imperial Household Rites Ordinance was abolished in 1947, the tradition continues as an Imperial Court rite. During the {{ill|Heisei period|ja|平成}}, the {{ill|Emperor Shōwa Festival|ja|昭和天皇祭}} was conducted, and following the abdication of Emperor Akihito on April 30, 2019, the Emperor Shōwa Festival continues as the most recent commemoration of a deceased emperor into the {{ill|Reiwa period|ja|令和}}.
On the day of Senteisai, it was previously designated a public holiday under the former laws "Holiday Law" and "Regulations on Holidays." However, it was removed from the list of holidays with the enactment of the current "Act on National Holidays" in 1948.
{| class="wikitable" |+ Senteisai after the Meiji period |- ! Era !! Senteisai !! Date |- | {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} || {{ill|Emperor Kōmei|ja|孝明天皇}} Festival || January 30 |- | {{ill|Taishō|ja|大正}} || {{ill|Emperor Meiji|ja|明治天皇}} Festival || July 30 |- | {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} || {{ill|Emperor Taishō|ja|大正天皇}} Festival || December 25 |- | {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} || Emperor Shōwa Festival || January 7 |- | {{ill|Reiwa|ja|令和}} || Emperor Shōwa Festival || January 7 |}
=== Akama Shrine (Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi Prefecture) Festival === Following the {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦の戦い}} in 1186 (Bunji 2), {{ill|Emperor Go-Toba|ja|後鳥羽天皇}} issued a decree to Nagato Province for the enshrinement of Emperor Antoku's remains at Amidaji Temple (now Akama Shrine). A commemorative festival called the "Senteikai" was held annually on his death anniversary (March 24), which later became the "Main Festival" on April 23 under the Gregorian calendar and was eventually moved to the current date of May 2.
On May 2, ceremonies such as the "Goryōmae Festival," attended by the {{ill|National Heike Association|ja|全国平家会}}, are held. On May 3, the "Nakajima Group's Pilgrimage Ceremony" follows, inspired by a story of loyalists visiting the shrine. This is succeeded by the "U11er-class Women's Procession" (Jōrō Dōchū), a tradition rooted in the courtesans and later geisha who honored Emperor Antoku. The procession features participants dressed in court attire, ste11ing in the traditional {{ill|Soto Hachimonji|ja|外八文字}} style through Shimonoseki.
The main festival concludes on May 4 with the {{ill|Shinko Festival|ja|神幸祭}}.
This festival, alongside the {{ill|Kokura Gion Festival|ja|小倉祇園祭}} across the Kanmon Straits in Kitakyushu, is known for the saying, "If it doesn't rain at Senteisai in Seki or Gion in Kokura, money will pour like rain," highlighting the lively crowds.
The festival is designated an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|無形民俗文化財}} of Shimonoseki City<ref>[http://www.stca-kanko.or.jp/sightseeing/city_summary/events/ Shimonoseki Tourism and Convention Association]</ref>.

The main festival concludes on May 4 with the {{ill|Shinko Festival|ja|神幸祭}}.

The festival is designated an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|無形民俗文化財}} of Shimonoseki City<ref>[http://www.stca-kanko.or.jp/sightseeing/city_summary/events/ Shimonoseki Tourism and Convention Association]</ref>.

[[File:下関豊前田商店街_先帝祭_上臈道中_壱.jpg|thumb| The Late Emperor's Festival: Procession of Ladies (Shimonoseki Toyomaeda Sho11ing Arcade)]] [[File:下関豊前田商店街_先帝祭_上臈道中_五.jpg|thumb| The Late Emperor's Festival: Procession of Ladies (Shimonoseki Toyomaeda Sho11ing Arcade)]] {{User:Immanuelle/Japanese Imperial Rituals updated}} '''Senteisai''' (先帝祭) is a festival commemorating the previous emperor.
== Classifications == Examples include: # {{ill|Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace|ja|宮中祭祀}} honoring the previous {{ill|Emperor of Japan|ja|天皇}}. These major festivals are held annually on the day of the late emperor's passing. # A festival held at {{ill|Akama Shrine|ja|赤間神宮}} in {{ill|Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi|ja|下関市}}, Yamaguchi Prefecture, from May 2 to May 4. In this case, the "previous emperor" specifically refers to {{ill|Emperor Antoku|ja|安徳天皇}}.
=== Imperial Court Rites === As one of the major rites defined by the {{ill|Imperial Household Rites Ordinance|ja|皇室祭祀令}}, it is held annually on the day of the previous emperor's passing. The actual name of the ritual replaces "Senteisai" with the posthumous title of the emperor.
Although the Imperial Household Rites Ordinance was abolished in 1947, the tradition continues as an Imperial Court rite. During the {{ill|Heisei period|ja|平成}}, the {{ill|Emperor Shōwa Festival|ja|昭和天皇祭}} was conducted, and following the abdication of Emperor Akihito on April 30, 2019, the Emperor Shōwa Festival continues as the most recent commemoration of a deceased emperor into the {{ill|Reiwa period|ja|令和}}.
On the day of Senteisai, it was previously designated a public holiday under the former laws "Holiday Law" and "Regulations on Holidays." However, it was removed from the list of holidays with the enactment of the current "Act on National Holidays" in 1948.
{| class="wikitable" |+ Senteisai after the Meiji period |- ! Era !! Senteisai !! Date |- | {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} || {{ill|Emperor Kōmei|ja|孝明天皇}} Festival || January 30 |- | {{ill|Taishō|ja|大正}} || {{ill|Emperor Meiji|ja|明治天皇}} Festival || July 30 |- | {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} || {{ill|Emperor Taishō|ja|大正天皇}} Festival || December 25 |- | {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} || Emperor Shōwa Festival || January 7 |- | {{ill|Reiwa|ja|令和}} || Emperor Shōwa Festival || January 7 |}
=== Akama Shrine (Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi Prefecture) Festival === Following the {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦の戦い}} in 1186 (Bunji 2), {{ill|Emperor Go-Toba|ja|後鳥羽天皇}} issued a decree to Nagato Province for the enshrinement of Emperor Antoku's remains at Amidaji Temple (now Akama Shrine). A commemorative festival called the "Senteikai" was held annually on his death anniversary (March 24), which later became the "Main Festival" on April 23 under the Gregorian calendar and was eventually moved to the current date of May 2.
On May 2, ceremonies such as the "Goryōmae Festival," attended by the {{ill|National Heike Association|ja|全国平家会}}, are held. On May 3, the "Nakajima Group's Pilgrimage Ceremony" follows, inspired by a story of loyalists visiting the shrine. This is succeeded by the "U11er-class Women's Procession" (Jōrō Dōchū), a tradition rooted in the courtesans and later geisha who honored Emperor Antoku. The procession features participants dressed in court attire, ste11ing in the traditional {{ill|Soto Hachimonji|ja|外八文字}} style through Shimonoseki.
The main festival concludes on May 4 with the {{ill|Shinko Festival|ja|神幸祭}}.
This festival, alongside the {{ill|Kokura Gion Festival|ja|小倉祇園祭}} across the Kanmon Straits in Kitakyushu, is known for the saying, "If it doesn't rain at Senteisai in Seki or Gion in Kokura, money will pour like rain," highlighting the lively crowds.
The festival is designated an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|無形民俗文化財}} of Shimonoseki City<ref>[http://www.stca-kanko.or.jp/sightseeing/city_summary/events/ Shimonoseki Tourism and Convention Association]</ref>.
== References == * 'Shinto Encyclopedia' (Heibonsha, reprinted by Rinsen Shoten, 1969), ISBN 4-653-01347-0.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Kokuki|ja|国忌}} * {{ill|Shimonoseki red-light district|ja|下関の遊廓}}
== External Links == * [http://www.tiki.ne.jp/~akama-jingu/senteisai.html Senteisai] (Akama Shrine Official Website)

Although the Imperial Household Rites Ordinance was abolished in 1947, the tradition continues as an Imperial Court rite. During the {{ill|Heisei period|ja|平成}}, the {{ill|Emperor Shōwa Festival|ja|昭和天皇祭}} was conducted, and following the abdication of Emperor Akihito on April 30, 2019, the Emperor Shōwa Festival continues as the most recent commemoration of a deceased emperor into the {{ill|Reiwa period|ja|令和}}.
On the day of Senteisai, it was previously designated a public holiday under the former laws "Holiday Law" and "Regulations on Holidays." However, it was removed from the list of holidays with the enactment of the current "Act on National Holidays" in 1948.
{| class="wikitable" |+ Senteisai after the Meiji period |- ! Era !! Senteisai !! Date |- | {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} || {{ill|Emperor Kōmei|ja|孝明天皇}} Festival || January 30 |- | {{ill|Taishō|ja|大正}} || {{ill|Emperor Meiji|ja|明治天皇}} Festival || July 30 |- | {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} || {{ill|Emperor Taishō|ja|大正天皇}} Festival || December 25 |- | {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} || Emperor Shōwa Festival || January 7 |- | {{ill|Reiwa|ja|令和}} || Emperor Shōwa Festival || January 7 |}
=== Akama Shrine (Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi Prefecture) Festival === Following the {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦の戦い}} in 1186 (Bunji 2), {{ill|Emperor Go-Toba|ja|後鳥羽天皇}} issued a decree to Nagato Province for the enshrinement of Emperor Antoku's remains at Amidaji Temple (now Akama Shrine). A commemorative festival called the "Senteikai" was held annually on his death anniversary (March 24), which later became the "Main Festival" on April 23 under the Gregorian calendar and was eventually moved to the current date of May 2.
On May 2, ceremonies such as the "Goryōmae Festival," attended by the {{ill|National Heike Association|ja|全国平家会}}, are held. On May 3, the "Nakajima Group's Pilgrimage Ceremony" follows, inspired by a story of loyalists visiting the shrine. This is succeeded by the "U11er-class Women's Procession" (Jōrō Dōchū), a tradition rooted in the courtesans and later geisha who honored Emperor Antoku. The procession features participants dressed in court attire, ste11ing in the traditional {{ill|Soto Hachimonji|ja|外八文字}} style through Shimonoseki.
The main festival concludes on May 4 with the {{ill|Shinko Festival|ja|神幸祭}}.
This festival, alongside the {{ill|Kokura Gion Festival|ja|小倉祇園祭}} across the Kanmon Straits in Kitakyushu, is known for the saying, "If it doesn't rain at Senteisai in Seki or Gion in Kokura, money will pour like rain," highlighting the lively crowds.
The festival is designated an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|無形民俗文化財}} of Shimonoseki City<ref>[http://www.stca-kanko.or.jp/sightseeing/city_summary/events/ Shimonoseki Tourism and Convention Association]</ref>.

=== Akama Shrine (Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi Prefecture) Festival === Following the {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦の戦い|12=draft}} in 1186 (Bunji 2), {{ill|Emperor Go-Toba|ja|後鳥羽天皇|12=draft}} issued a decree to Nagato Province for the enshrinement of Emperor Antoku's remains at Amidaji Temple (now Akama Shrine). A commemorative festival called the "Senteikai" was held annually on his death anniversary (March 24), which later became the "Main Festival" on April 23 under the Gregorian calendar and was eventually moved to the current date of May 2.
On May 2, ceremonies such as the "Goryōmae Festival," attended by the {{ill|National Heike Association|ja|全国平家会|12=draft}}, are held. On May 3, the "Nakajima Group's Pilgrimage Ceremony" follows, inspired by a story of loyalists visiting the shrine. This is succeeded by the "U11er-class Women's Procession" (Jōrō Dōchū), a tradition rooted in the courtesans and later geisha who honored Emperor Antoku. The procession features participants dressed in court attire, ste11ing in the traditional {{ill|Soto Hachimonji|ja|外八文字|12=draft}} style through Shimonoseki.
The main festival concludes on May 4 with the {{ill|Shinko Festival|ja|神幸祭|12=draft}}.
This festival, alongside the {{ill|Kokura Gion Festival|ja|小倉祇園祭|12=draft}} across the Kanmon Straits in Kitakyushu, is known for the saying, "If it doesn't rain at Senteisai in Seki or Gion in Kokura, money will pour like rain," highlighting the lively crowds.
The festival is designated an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|無形民俗文化財|12=draft}} of Shimonoseki City<ref>[http://www.stca-kanko.or.jp/sightseeing/city_summary/events/ Shimonoseki Tourism and Convention Association]</ref>.
== References == * 'Shinto Encyclopedia' (Heibonsha, reprinted by Rinsen Shoten, 1969), ISBN 4-653-01347-0.

[[File:下関豊前田商店街_先帝祭_上臈道中_壱.jpg|thumb| The Late Emperor's Festival: Procession of Ladies (Shimonoseki Toyomaeda Sho11ing Arcade)]] [[File:下関豊前田商店街_先帝祭_上臈道中_五.jpg|thumb| The Late Emperor's Festival: Procession of Ladies (Shimonoseki Toyomaeda Sho11ing Arcade)]] {{User:Immanuelle/Japanese Imperial Rituals updated}} '''Senteisai''' (先帝祭) is a festival commemorating the previous emperor.
== Classifications == Examples include: # {{ill|Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace|ja|宮中祭祀|12=draft}} honoring the previous {{ill|Emperor of Japan|ja|天皇|12=draft}}. These major festivals are held annually on the day of the late emperor's passing. # A festival held at {{ill|Akama Shrine|ja|赤間神宮|12=draft}} in {{ill|Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi|ja|下関市|12=draft}}, Yamaguchi Prefecture, from May 2 to May 4. In this case, the "previous emperor" specifically refers to {{ill|Emperor Antoku|ja|安徳天皇|12=draft}}.
=== Imperial Court Rites === As one of the major rites defined by the {{ill|Imperial Household Rites Ordinance|ja|皇室祭祀令|12=draft}}, it is held annually on the day of the previous emperor's passing. The actual name of the ritual replaces "Senteisai" with the posthumous title of the emperor.
Although the Imperial Household Rites Ordinance was abolished in 1947, the tradition continues as an Imperial Court rite. During the {{ill|Heisei period|ja|平成|12=draft}}, the {{ill|Emperor Shōwa Festival|ja|昭和天皇祭|12=draft}} was conducted, and following the abdication of Emperor Akihito on April 30, 2019, the Emperor Shōwa Festival continues as the most recent commemoration of a deceased emperor into the {{ill|Reiwa period|ja|令和|12=draft}}.
On the day of Senteisai, it was previously designated a public holiday under the former laws "Holiday Law" and "Regulations on Holidays." However, it was removed from the list of holidays with the enactment of the current "Act on National Holidays" in 1948.

The main festival concludes on May 4 with the {{ill|Shinko Festival|ja|神幸祭|12=draft}}.

The festival is designated an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|無形民俗文化財|12=draft}} of Shimonoseki City<ref>[http://www.stca-kanko.or.jp/sightseeing/city_summary/events/ Shimonoseki Tourism and Convention Association]</ref>.

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
[[File:下関豊前田商店街_先帝祭_上臈道中_壱.jpg|thumb| The Late Emperor's Festival: Procession of Ladies (Shimonoseki Toyomaeda Sho11ing Arcade)]] [[File:下関豊前田商店街_先帝祭_上臈道中_五.jpg|thumb| The Late Emperor's Festival: Procession of Ladies (Shimonoseki Toyomaeda Sho11ing Arcade)]] {{User:Immanuelle/Japanese Imperial Rituals updated}} '''Senteisai''' (先帝祭) is a festival commemorating the previous emperor.
== Classifications == Examples include: # {{ill|Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace|ja|宮中祭祀|12=draft}} honoring the previous {{ill|Emperor of Japan|ja|天皇|12=draft}}. These major festivals are held annually on the day of the late emperor's passing. # A festival held at {{ill|Akama Shrine|ja|赤間神宮|12=draft}} in {{ill|Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi|ja|下関市|12=draft}}, Yamaguchi Prefecture, from May 2 to May 4. In this case, the "previous emperor" specifically refers to {{ill|Emperor Antoku|ja|安徳天皇|12=draft}}.
=== Imperial Court Rites === As one of the major rites defined by the {{ill|Imperial Household Rites Ordinance|ja|皇室祭祀令|12=draft}}, it is held annually on the day of the previous emperor's passing. The actual name of the ritual replaces "Senteisai" with the posthumous title of the emperor.
Although the Imperial Household Rites Ordinance was abolished in 1947, the tradition continues as an Imperial Court rite. During the {{ill|Heisei period|ja|平成|12=draft}}, the {{ill|Emperor Shōwa Festival|ja|昭和天皇祭|12=draft}} was conducted, and following the abdication of Emperor Akihito on April 30, 2019, the Emperor Shōwa Festival continues as the most recent commemoration of a deceased emperor into the {{ill|Reiwa period|ja|令和|12=draft}}.
On the day of Senteisai, it was previously designated a public holiday under the former laws "Holiday Law" and "Regulations on Holidays." However, it was removed from the list of holidays with the enactment of the current "Act on National Holidays" in 1948.
{| class="wikitable" |+ Senteisai after the Meiji period |- ! Era !! Senteisai !! Date |- | {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft}} || {{ill|Emperor Kōmei|ja|孝明天皇|12=draft}} Festival || January 30 |- | {{ill|Taishō|ja|大正|12=draft}} || {{ill|Emperor Meiji|ja|明治天皇|12=draft}} Festival || July 30 |- | {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和|12=draft}} || {{ill|Emperor Taishō|ja|大正天皇|12=draft}} Festival || December 25 |- | {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成|12=draft}} || Emperor Shōwa Festival || January 7 |- | {{ill|Reiwa|ja|令和|12=draft}} || Emperor Shōwa Festival || January 7 |}
=== Akama Shrine (Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi Prefecture) Festival === Following the {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦の戦い|12=draft}} in 1186 (Bunji 2), {{ill|Emperor Go-Toba|ja|後鳥羽天皇|12=draft}} issued a decree to Nagato Province for the enshrinement of Emperor Antoku's remains at Amidaji Temple (now Akama Shrine). A commemorative festival called the "Senteikai" was held annually on his death anniversary (March 24), which later became the "Main Festival" on April 23 under the Gregorian calendar and was eventually moved to the current date of May 2.
On May 2, ceremonies such as the "Goryōmae Festival," attended by the {{ill|National Heike Association|ja|全国平家会|12=draft}}, are held. On May 3, the "Nakajima Group's Pilgrimage Ceremony" follows, inspired by a story of loyalists visiting the shrine. This is succeeded by the "U11er-class Women's Procession" (Jōrō Dōchū), a tradition rooted in the courtesans and later geisha who honored Emperor Antoku. The procession features participants dressed in court attire, ste11ing in the traditional {{ill|Soto Hachimonji|ja|外八文字|12=draft}} style through Shimonoseki.
The main festival concludes on May 4 with the {{ill|Shinko Festival|ja|神幸祭|12=draft}}.
This festival, alongside the {{ill|Kokura Gion Festival|ja|小倉祇園祭|12=draft}} across the Kanmon Straits in Kitakyushu, is known for the saying, "If it doesn't rain at Senteisai in Seki or Gion in Kokura, money will pour like rain," highlighting the lively crowds.
The festival is designated an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|無形民俗文化財|12=draft}} of Shimonoseki City<ref>[http://www.stca-kanko.or.jp/sightseeing/city_summary/events/ Shimonoseki Tourism and Convention Association]</ref>.
== References == * 'Shinto Encyclopedia' (Heibonsha, reprinted by Rinsen Shoten, 1969), ISBN 4-653-01347-0.

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|安徳天皇|" to "|安徳天皇|1=Emperor Antoku|2=ja|3=安徳天皇|4=de|5=Antoku|"
revision.text:
[[File:下関豊前田商店街_先帝祭_上臈道中_壱.jpg|thumb| The Late Emperor's Festival: Procession of Ladies (Shimonoseki Toyomaeda Sho11ing Arcade)]] [[File:下関豊前田商店街_先帝祭_上臈道中_五.jpg|thumb| The Late Emperor's Festival: Procession of Ladies (Shimonoseki Toyomaeda Sho11ing Arcade)]] {{User:Immanuelle/Japanese Imperial Rituals updated}} '''Senteisai''' (先帝祭) is a festival commemorating the previous emperor.
== Classifications == Examples include: # {{ill|Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace|ja|宮中祭祀|12=draft}} honoring the previous {{ill|Emperor of Japan|ja|天皇|12=draft}}. These major festivals are held annually on the day of the late emperor's passing. # A festival held at {{ill|Akama Shrine|ja|赤間神宮|12=draft}} in {{ill|Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi|ja|下関市|12=draft}}, Yamaguchi Prefecture, from May 2 to May 4. In this case, the "previous emperor" specifically refers to {{ill|Emperor Antoku|ja|安徳天皇|1=Emperor Antoku|2=ja|3=安徳天皇|4=de|5=Antoku|12=draft}}.
=== Imperial Court Rites === As one of the major rites defined by the {{ill|Imperial Household Rites Ordinance|ja|皇室祭祀令|12=draft}}, it is held annually on the day of the previous emperor's passing. The actual name of the ritual replaces "Senteisai" with the posthumous title of the emperor.
Although the Imperial Household Rites Ordinance was abolished in 1947, the tradition continues as an Imperial Court rite. During the {{ill|Heisei period|ja|平成|12=draft}}, the {{ill|Emperor Shōwa Festival|ja|昭和天皇祭|12=draft}} was conducted, and following the abdication of Emperor Akihito on April 30, 2019, the Emperor Shōwa Festival continues as the most recent commemoration of a deceased emperor into the {{ill|Reiwa period|ja|令和|12=draft}}.
On the day of Senteisai, it was previously designated a public holiday under the former laws "Holiday Law" and "Regulations on Holidays." However, it was removed from the list of holidays with the enactment of the current "Act on National Holidays" in 1948.
{| class="wikitable" |+ Senteisai after the Meiji period |- ! Era !! Senteisai !! Date |- | {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft}} || {{ill|Emperor Kōmei|ja|孝明天皇|12=draft}} Festival || January 30 |- | {{ill|Taishō|ja|大正|12=draft}} || {{ill|Emperor Meiji|ja|明治天皇|12=draft}} Festival || July 30 |- | {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和|12=draft}} || {{ill|Emperor Taishō|ja|大正天皇|12=draft}} Festival || December 25 |- | {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成|12=draft}} || Emperor Shōwa Festival || January 7 |- | {{ill|Reiwa|ja|令和|12=draft}} || Emperor Shōwa Festival || January 7 |}
=== Akama Shrine (Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi Prefecture) Festival === Following the {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦の戦い|12=draft}} in 1186 (Bunji 2), {{ill|Emperor Go-Toba|ja|後鳥羽天皇|12=draft}} issued a decree to Nagato Province for the enshrinement of Emperor Antoku's remains at Amidaji Temple (now Akama Shrine). A commemorative festival called the "Senteikai" was held annually on his death anniversary (March 24), which later became the "Main Festival" on April 23 under the Gregorian calendar and was eventually moved to the current date of May 2.
On May 2, ceremonies such as the "Goryōmae Festival," attended by the {{ill|National Heike Association|ja|全国平家会|12=draft}}, are held. On May 3, the "Nakajima Group's Pilgrimage Ceremony" follows, inspired by a story of loyalists visiting the shrine. This is succeeded by the "U11er-class Women's Procession" (Jōrō Dōchū), a tradition rooted in the courtesans and later geisha who honored Emperor Antoku. The procession features participants dressed in court attire, ste11ing in the traditional {{ill|Soto Hachimonji|ja|外八文字|12=draft}} style through Shimonoseki.
The main festival concludes on May 4 with the {{ill|Shinko Festival|ja|神幸祭|12=draft}}.
This festival, alongside the {{ill|Kokura Gion Festival|ja|小倉祇園祭|12=draft}} across the Kanmon Straits in Kitakyushu, is known for the saying, "If it doesn't rain at Senteisai in Seki or Gion in Kokura, money will pour like rain," highlighting the lively crowds.
The festival is designated an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|無形民俗文化財|12=draft}} of Shimonoseki City<ref>[http://www.stca-kanko.or.jp/sightseeing/city_summary/events/ Shimonoseki Tourism and Convention Association]</ref>.
== References == * 'Shinto Encyclopedia' (Heibonsha, reprinted by Rinsen Shoten, 1969), ISBN 4-653-01347-0.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Kokuki|ja|国忌|12=draft}} * {{ill|Shimonoseki red-light district|ja|下関の遊廓|12=draft}}
== External Links == * [http://www.tiki.ne.jp/~akama-jingu/senteisai.html Senteisai] (Akama Shrine Official Website)

revision.text:
[[File:下関豊前田商店街 先帝祭 上臈道中 壱.jpg|thumb|先帝祭 上臈道中（下関豊前田商店街）]] [[File:下関豊前田商店街 先帝祭 上臈道中 五.jpg|thumb|先帝祭 上臈道中（下関豊前田商店街）]] {{皇室祭祀}} '''先帝祭'''（せんていさい）は、先帝を偲ぶ祭り。
== 分類 == 以下の例がある。 #先代の[[天皇]]を祭る[[宮中祭祀]]。先帝[[崩御]]日に毎年斎行される大祭。 #[[山口県]][[下関市]]にある[[赤間神宮]]の祭事。毎年[[5月2日]]から[[5月4日]]にかけて行われる。この場合の先帝は特定の歴代天皇、[[安徳天皇]]を指す。
=== 宮中祭祀 === [[皇室祭祀令]]が定める大祭のひとつで、先帝崩御日に毎年斎行される。実際の祭祀の名称は先帝祭ではなく、先帝の箇所に天皇[[諡号]]が入る。
[[1947年]]（[[昭和]]22年）に皇室祭祀令は廃止されたが、以降も宮中祭祀として同様に斎行されている。[[平成]]時代は[[昭和天皇]]祭が斎行されてきたが、[[2019年]]（平成31年）4月30日の第125代天皇[[明仁]]の[[退位]]により、先帝ではなく直近の崩御した歴代天皇の祭祀として昭和天皇祭が[[令和]]時代も継続される。
先帝祭当日は、かつての休日法「[[年中祭日祝日ノ休暇日ヲ定ム]]」および「[[休日ニ関スル件]]」で[[休日]]となっていたが、[[1948年]]（昭和23年）に施行された現行の休日法「[[国民の祝日に関する法律]]」で休日から外された。
{| class="wikitable" |+ 明治以後の先帝祭 |- !元号!!先帝祭!!日付 |- |[[明治]]||[[孝明天皇]]祭||[[1月30日]] |- |[[大正]]||[[明治天皇]]祭||[[7月30日]] |- |[[昭和]]||[[大正天皇]]祭||[[12月25日]] |- |[[平成]]||[[昭和天皇]]祭||[[1月7日]] |- |[[令和]]||昭和天皇祭||1月7日 |}
=== 赤間神宮（山口県下関市）の祭事 === [[壇ノ浦の戦い]]の翌[[文治]]2年（[[1186年]]）、[[後鳥羽天皇]]が[[長門国]]に勅して安徳天皇の遺骸を納めた阿弥陀寺（現赤間神宮）境内に御影堂を建立し、安徳天皇の命日（3月24日）にその遺徳を偲ぶ'''先帝会'''を修したのが起源といい、以後毎年3月24日に安徳天皇の[[御陵]]の前で「御陵前祭」を営み、[[明治]]以降は祭日を[[太陽暦]]（4月23日）に改めて[[例祭]]とし、その後更に現行日（5月2日）へと改めた。
2日は平家落人の子孫らで組織される[[全国平家会]]の参列のもと御陵前での神事を始め、平家一門追悼祭などがある。 翌3日に、[[平家]]の遺臣で中島四郎太夫という者が、漁師に身を窶して平家再興を計りつつ下関に潜伏、先帝会には威儀を正して参拝したという故事に因んで、その子孫に端を発する「中島組」という漁業団体員が参拝、これを「中島組参拝の式」という。それに次いで「上臈・官女参拝の式（「上臈道中」）」となるが、これも壇ノ浦の戦いの後に在地の苫屋に援われた[[平徳子|建礼門院（平徳子）]]の侍女達が、在地の苫屋に養われつつ御陵に香花を手向け、先帝会には容姿を整えて参拝したことに縁由するという。その後[[遊廓|妓楼]]を営むようになった苫屋の主人が、侍女達及びその遺族も没したために、抱える[[遊女]]達にその風俗を真似て宮廷装束に身を纏わせて参拝させるようになり、これが[[江戸時代]]に至って、当時存在した[[下関の遊廓|稲荷町遊廓]]の遊女によって受け継がれて、現在の「上臈道中（じょうろうどうちゅう）」となったといい、[[吉原遊廓|吉原]]の[[花魁]]に模した[[太夫]]が[[禿]]（かむろ）、[[上臈]]、[[稚児]]、警固（けいご）らを従え、下関市中を[[外八文字]]を踏んで歩く。
例祭の翌々4日には[[神幸祭]]が行われる。
関門海峡を挟んで対岸となる福岡県北九州市の小倉祇園祭りとこの祭りは、昔から「関の先帝、小倉の祇園雨が降らなきゃ金が降る」と雨が降らないと大勢の人々が祭りに繰り出してたいそうな賑わいとなる模様をこう言った。
下関市指定[[無形民俗文化財]]に指定されている<ref>[http://www.stca-kanko.or.jp/sightseeing/city_summary/events/ 下関観光コンベンション協会]</ref>。
== 参考文献 == *『神道大辞典』（[[平凡社]]、昭和14年刊の縮刷復刻版）、[[臨川書店]]、昭和44年 ISBN 4-653-01347-0
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} <references />
== 関連項目 == *[[国忌]] *[[下関の遊廓]]
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.tiki.ne.jp/~akama-jingu/senteisai.html 先帝祭]（赤間神宮公式）
{{japanese-history-stub}} {{japan-culture-stub}} {{event-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=山口県}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:せんていさい}} [[Category:下関市の文化]] [[Category:山口県の祭り]] [[Category:神道行事]] [[Category:皇室の儀式]] [[Category:区市町村指定無形民俗文化財]]

[[File:下関豊前田商店街_先帝祭_上臈道中_壱.jpg|thumb| The Late Emperor's Festival: Procession of Ladies (Shimonoseki Toyomaeda Sho11ing Arcade)]] [[File:下関豊前田商店街_先帝祭_上臈道中_五.jpg|thumb| The Late Emperor's Festival: Procession of Ladies (Shimonoseki Toyomaeda Sho11ing Arcade)]] {{User:Immanuelle/Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace updated}} '''Senteisai''' (先帝祭) is a festival commemorating the previous emperor.
== Classifications == Examples include: # [[:en:Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace|Ceremonies of the Japanese Imperial Palace]] honoring the previous [[:en:Emperor of Japan|Emperor of Japan]]. These major festivals are held annually on the day of the late emperor's passing. # A festival held at [[:en:Akama Shrine|Akama Shrine]] in [[:en:Shimonoseki|Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi]], Yamaguchi Prefecture, from May 2 to May 4. In this case, the "previous emperor" specifically refers to [[:en:Emperor Antoku|Emperor Antoku]].
=== Imperial Court Rites === As one of the major rites defined by the {{ill|Imperial Household Rites Act|ja|皇室祭祀令|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Rites Ordinance|qq=}}, it is held annually on the day of the previous emperor's passing. The actual name of the ritual replaces "Senteisai" with the posthumous title of the emperor.
Although the Imperial Household Rites Ordinance was abolished in 1947, the tradition continues as an Imperial Court rite. During the [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei period]], the {{ill|Emperor Shōwa Festival|ja|昭和天皇祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Shōwa Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was conducted, and following the abdication of Emperor Akihito on April 30, 2019, the Emperor Shōwa Festival continues as the most recent commemoration of a deceased emperor into the [[:en:Reiwa era|Reiwa period]].
On the day of Senteisai, it was previously designated a public holiday under the former laws "Holiday Law" and "Regulations on Holidays." However, it was removed from the list of holidays with the enactment of the current "Act on National Holidays" in 1948.

=== Akama Shrine (Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi Prefecture) Festival === Following the [[:en:Battle of Dan-no-ura|Battle of Dan-no-ura]] in 1186 (Bunji 2), [[:en:Emperor Go-Toba|Emperor Go-Toba]] issued a decree to Nagato Province for the enshrinement of Emperor Antoku's remains at Amidaji Temple (now Akama Shrine). A commemorative festival called the "Senteikai" was held annually on his death anniversary (March 24), which later became the "Main Festival" on April 23 under the Gregorian calendar and was eventually moved to the current date of May 2.
On May 2, ceremonies such as the "Goryōmae Festival," attended by the {{ill|National Heike Association|ja|全国平家会|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Heike Association|qq=}}, are held. On May 3, the "Nakajima Group's Pilgrimage Ceremony" follows, inspired by a story of loyalists visiting the shrine. This is succeeded by the "U11er-class Women's Procession" (Jōrō Dōchū), a tradition rooted in the courtesans and later geisha who honored Emperor Antoku. The procession features participants dressed in court attire, ste11ing in the traditional {{ill|Soto Hachimonji|ja|外八文字|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soto Hachimonji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} style through Shimonoseki.
The main festival concludes on May 4 with the [[:en:Shinko-shiki|Shinko Festival]].
This festival, alongside the {{ill|Kokura Gion Festival|ja|小倉祇園祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokura Gion Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} across the Kanmon Straits in Kitakyushu, is known for the saying, "If it doesn't rain at Senteisai in Seki or Gion in Kokura, money will pour like rain," highlighting the lively crowds.
The festival is designated an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 民俗文化財}} of Shimonoseki City<ref>[http://www.stca-kanko.or.jp/sightseeing/city_summary/events/ Shimonoseki Tourism and Convention Association]</ref>.

revision.text:
[[File:下関豊前田商店街_先帝祭_上臈道中_壱.jpg|thumb| The Late Emperor's Festival: Procession of Ladies (Shimonoseki Toyomaeda Sho11ing Arcade)]] [[File:下関豊前田商店街_先帝祭_上臈道中_五.jpg|thumb| The Late Emperor's Festival: Procession of Ladies (Shimonoseki Toyomaeda Sho11ing Arcade)]] {{User:Immanuelle/Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace updated}} '''Senteisai''' (先帝祭) is a festival commemorating the previous emperor.
== Classifications == Examples include: # [[:en:Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace|Ceremonies of the Japanese Imperial Palace]] honoring the previous [[:en:Emperor of Japan|Emperor of Japan]]. These major festivals are held annually on the day of the late emperor's passing. # A festival held at [[:en:Akama Shrine|Akama Shrine]] in [[:en:Shimonoseki|Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi]], Yamaguchi Prefecture, from May 2 to May 4. In this case, the "previous emperor" specifically refers to [[:en:Emperor Antoku|Emperor Antoku]].
=== Imperial Court Rites === As one of the major rites defined by the {{ill|Imperial Household Rites Act|ja|皇室祭祀令|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Rites Ordinance|qq=}}, it is held annually on the day of the previous emperor's passing. The actual name of the ritual replaces "Senteisai" with the posthumous title of the emperor.
Although the Imperial Household Rites Ordinance was abolished in 1947, the tradition continues as an Imperial Court rite. During the [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei period]], the {{ill|Emperor Shōwa Festival|ja|昭和天皇祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Shōwa Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was conducted, and following the abdication of Emperor Akihito on April 30, 2019, the Emperor Shōwa Festival continues as the most recent commemoration of a deceased emperor into the [[:en:Reiwa era|Reiwa period]].
On the day of Senteisai, it was previously designated a public holiday under the former laws "Holiday Law" and "Regulations on Holidays." However, it was removed from the list of holidays with the enactment of the current "Act on National Holidays" in 1948.
{| class="wikitable" |+ Senteisai after the Meiji period |- ! Era !! Senteisai !! Date |- | [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] || [[:en:Emperor Kōmei|Emperor Kōmei]] Festival || January 30 |- | [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] || [[:en:Emperor Meiji|Emperor Meiji]] Festival || July 30 |- | [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] || [[:en:Emperor Taishō|Emperor Taishō]] Festival || December 25 |- | [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] || Emperor Shōwa Festival || January 7 |- | [[:en:Reiwa era|Reiwa]] || Emperor Shōwa Festival || January 7 |}
=== Akama Shrine (Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi Prefecture) Festival === Following the [[:en:Battle of Dan-no-ura|Battle of Dan-no-ura]] in 1186 (Bunji 2), [[:en:Emperor Go-Toba|Emperor Go-Toba]] issued a decree to Nagato Province for the enshrinement of Emperor Antoku's remains at Amidaji Temple (now Akama Shrine). A commemorative festival called the "Senteikai" was held annually on his death anniversary (March 24), which later became the "Main Festival" on April 23 under the Gregorian calendar and was eventually moved to the current date of May 2.
On May 2, ceremonies such as the "Goryōmae Festival," attended by the {{ill|National Heike Association|ja|全国平家会|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Heike Association|qq=}}, are held. On May 3, the "Nakajima Group's Pilgrimage Ceremony" follows, inspired by a story of loyalists visiting the shrine. This is succeeded by the "U11er-class Women's Procession" (Jōrō Dōchū), a tradition rooted in the courtesans and later geisha who honored Emperor Antoku. The procession features participants dressed in court attire, ste11ing in the traditional {{ill|Soto Hachimonji|ja|外八文字|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soto Hachimonji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} style through Shimonoseki.
The main festival concludes on May 4 with the [[:en:Shinko-shiki|Shinko Festival]].
This festival, alongside the {{ill|Kokura Gion Festival|ja|小倉祇園祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokura Gion Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} across the Kanmon Straits in Kitakyushu, is known for the saying, "If it doesn't rain at Senteisai in Seki or Gion in Kokura, money will pour like rain," highlighting the lively crowds.
The festival is designated an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 民俗文化財}} of Shimonoseki City<ref>[http://www.stca-kanko.or.jp/sightseeing/city_summary/events/ Shimonoseki Tourism and Convention Association]</ref>.
== References == * 'Shinto Encyclopedia' (Heibonsha, reprinted by Rinsen Shoten, 1969), ISBN 4-653-01347-0.

=== Akama Shrine (Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi Prefecture) Festival === Following the [[:en:Battle of Dan-no-ura|Battle of Dan-no-ura]] in 1186 (Bunji 2), [[:en:Emperor Go-Toba|Emperor Go-Toba]] issued a decree to Nagato Province for the enshrinement of Emperor Antoku's remains at Amidaji Temple (now Akama Shrine). A commemorative festival called the "Senteikai" was held annually on his death anniversary (March 24), which later became the "Main Festival" on April 23 under the Gregorian calendar and was eventually moved to the current date of May 2.
On May 2, ceremonies such as the "Goryōmae Festival," attended by the {{ill|National Heike Association|ja|全国平家会|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Heike Association|qq=|1=National Heike Association|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11389374}}, are held. On May 3, the "Nakajima Group's Pilgrimage Ceremony" follows, inspired by a story of loyalists visiting the shrine. This is succeeded by the "U11er-class Women's Procession" (Jōrō Dōchū), a tradition rooted in the courtesans and later geisha who honored Emperor Antoku. The procession features participants dressed in court attire, ste11ing in the traditional {{ill|Soto Hachimonji|ja|外八文字|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soto Hachimonji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} style through Shimonoseki.
The main festival concludes on May 4 with the [[:en:Shinko-shiki|Shinko Festival]].
This festival, alongside the {{ill|Kokura Gion Festival|ja|小倉祇園祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokura Gion Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} across the Kanmon Straits in Kitakyushu, is known for the saying, "If it doesn't rain at Senteisai in Seki or Gion in Kokura, money will pour like rain," highlighting the lively crowds.
The festival is designated an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 民俗文化財|1=Folk Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24895900}} of Shimonoseki City<ref>[http://www.stca-kanko.or.jp/sightseeing/city_summary/events/ Shimonoseki Tourism and Convention Association]</ref>.
