<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:先帝祭]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11388139]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:53, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:下関豊前田商店街_先帝祭_上臈道中_壱.jpg|thumb| The Late Emperor's Festival: Procession of Ladies (Shimonoseki Toyomaeda Sho11ing Arcade)]] [[File:下関豊前田商店街_先帝祭_上臈道中_五.jpg|thumb| The Late Emperor's Festival: Procession of Ladies (Shimonoseki Toyomaeda Sho11ing Arcade)]] {{User:Immanuelle/Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace updated}} '''Senteisai''' (先帝祭) is a festival commemorating the previous emperor.
== Classifications == Examples include: # [[:en:Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace|Ceremonies of the Japanese Imperial Palace]] honoring the previous [[:en:Emperor of Japan|Emperor of Japan]]. These major festivals are held annually on the day of the late emperor's passing. # A festival held at [[:en:Akama Shrine|Akama Shrine]] in [[:en:Shimonoseki|Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi]], Yamaguchi Prefecture, from May 2 to May 4. In this case, the "previous emperor" specifically refers to [[:en:Emperor Antoku|Emperor Antoku]].
=== Imperial Court Rites === As one of the major rites defined by the {{ill|Imperial Household Rites Act|ja|皇室祭祀令|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Rites Ordinance|qq=}}, it is held annually on the day of the previous emperor's passing. The actual name of the ritual replaces "Senteisai" with the posthumous title of the emperor.
Although the Imperial Household Rites Ordinance was abolished in 1947, the tradition continues as an Imperial Court rite. During the [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei period]], the {{ill|Emperor Shōwa Festival|ja|昭和天皇祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Shōwa Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was conducted, and following the abdication of Emperor Akihito on April 30, 2019, the Emperor Shōwa Festival continues as the most recent commemoration of a deceased emperor into the [[:en:Reiwa era|Reiwa period]].
On the day of Senteisai, it was previously designated a public holiday under the former laws "Holiday Law" and "Regulations on Holidays." However, it was removed from the list of holidays with the enactment of the current "Act on National Holidays" in 1948.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:先帝祭]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11388139]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:53, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:下関豊前田商店街_先帝祭_上臈道中_壱.jpg|thumb| The Late Emperor's Festival: Procession of Ladies (Shimonoseki Toyomaeda Sho11ing Arcade)]] [[File:下関豊前田商店街_先帝祭_上臈道中_五.jpg|thumb| The Late Emperor's Festival: Procession of Ladies (Shimonoseki Toyomaeda Sho11ing Arcade)]] {{User:Immanuelle/Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace updated}} '''Senteisai''' (先帝祭) is a festival commemorating the previous emperor.
== Classifications == Examples include: # [[:en:Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace|Ceremonies of the Japanese Imperial Palace]] honoring the previous [[:en:Emperor of Japan|Emperor of Japan]]. These major festivals are held annually on the day of the late emperor's passing. # A festival held at [[:en:Akama Shrine|Akama Shrine]] in [[:en:Shimonoseki|Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi]], Yamaguchi Prefecture, from May 2 to May 4. In this case, the "previous emperor" specifically refers to [[:en:Emperor Antoku|Emperor Antoku]].
=== Imperial Court Rites === As one of the major rites defined by the {{ill|Imperial Household Rites Act|ja|皇室祭祀令|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Rites Ordinance|qq=}}, it is held annually on the day of the previous emperor's passing. The actual name of the ritual replaces "Senteisai" with the posthumous title of the emperor.
Although the Imperial Household Rites Ordinance was abolished in 1947, the tradition continues as an Imperial Court rite. During the [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei period]], the {{ill|Emperor Shōwa Festival|ja|昭和天皇祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Shōwa Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was conducted, and following the abdication of Emperor Akihito on April 30, 2019, the Emperor Shōwa Festival continues as the most recent commemoration of a deceased emperor into the [[:en:Reiwa era|Reiwa period]].
On the day of Senteisai, it was previously designated a public holiday under the former laws "Holiday Law" and "Regulations on Holidays." However, it was removed from the list of holidays with the enactment of the current "Act on National Holidays" in 1948.
{| class="wikitable" |+ Senteisai after the Meiji period |- ! Era !! Senteisai !! Date |- | [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] || [[:en:Emperor Kōmei|Emperor Kōmei]] Festival || January 30 |- | [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] || [[:en:Emperor Meiji|Emperor Meiji]] Festival || July 30 |- | [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] || [[:en:Emperor Taishō|Emperor Taishō]] Festival || December 25 |- | [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] || Emperor Shōwa Festival || January 7 |- | [[:en:Reiwa era|Reiwa]] || Emperor Shōwa Festival || January 7 |}
=== Akama Shrine (Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi Prefecture) Festival === Following the [[:en:Battle of Dan-no-ura|Battle of Dan-no-ura]] in 1186 (Bunji 2), [[:en:Emperor Go-Toba|Emperor Go-Toba]] issued a decree to Nagato Province for the enshrinement of Emperor Antoku's remains at Amidaji Temple (now Akama Shrine). A commemorative festival called the "Senteikai" was held annually on his death anniversary (March 24), which later became the "Main Festival" on April 23 under the Gregorian calendar and was eventually moved to the current date of May 2.
On May 2, ceremonies such as the "Goryōmae Festival," attended by the {{ill|National Heike Association|ja|全国平家会|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Heike Association|1=National Heike Association|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11389374}}, are held. On May 3, the "Nakajima Group's Pilgrimage Ceremony" follows, inspired by a story of loyalists visiting the shrine. This is succeeded by the "U11er-class Women's Procession" (Jōrō Dōchū), a tradition rooted in the courtesans and later geisha who honored Emperor Antoku. The procession features participants dressed in court attire, ste11ing in the traditional {{ill|Soto Hachimonji|ja|外八文字|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soto Hachimonji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} style through Shimonoseki.
The main festival concludes on May 4 with the [[:en:Shinko-shiki|Shinko Festival]].
This festival, alongside the {{ill|Kokura Gion Festival|ja|小倉祇園祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokura Gion Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} across the Kanmon Straits in Kitakyushu, is known for the saying, "If it doesn't rain at Senteisai in Seki or Gion in Kokura, money will pour like rain," highlighting the lively crowds.
The festival is designated an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 民俗文化財|1=Folk Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24895900}} of Shimonoseki City<ref>[http://www.stca-kanko.or.jp/sightseeing/city_summary/events/ Shimonoseki Tourism and Convention Association]</ref>.
== References == * 'Shinto Encyclopedia' (Heibonsha, reprinted by Rinsen Shoten, 1969), ISBN 4-653-01347-0.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:先帝祭]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11388139]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:53, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:下関豊前田商店街_先帝祭_上臈道中_壱.jpg|thumb| The Late Emperor's Festival: Procession of Ladies (Shimonoseki Toyomaeda Sho11ing Arcade)]] [[File:下関豊前田商店街_先帝祭_上臈道中_五.jpg|thumb| The Late Emperor's Festival: Procession of Ladies (Shimonoseki Toyomaeda Sho11ing Arcade)]] {{User:Immanuelle/Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace updated}} '''Senteisai''' (先帝祭) is a festival commemorating the previous emperor.
== Classifications == Examples include: # [[:en:Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace|Ceremonies of the Japanese Imperial Palace]] honoring the previous [[:en:Emperor of Japan|Emperor of Japan]]. These major festivals are held annually on the day of the late emperor's passing. # A festival held at [[:en:Akama Shrine|Akama Shrine]] in [[:en:Shimonoseki|Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi]], Yamaguchi Prefecture, from May 2 to May 4. In this case, the "previous emperor" specifically refers to [[:en:Emperor Antoku|Emperor Antoku]].
=== Imperial Court Rites === As one of the major rites defined by the {{ill|Imperial Household Rites Act|ja|皇室祭祀令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Rites Ordinance|qq=}}, it is held annually on the day of the previous emperor's passing. The actual name of the ritual replaces "Senteisai" with the posthumous title of the emperor.
Although the Imperial Household Rites Ordinance was abolished in 1947, the tradition continues as an Imperial Court rite. During the [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei period]], the {{ill|Emperor Shōwa Festival|ja|昭和天皇祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Shōwa Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was conducted, and following the abdication of Emperor Akihito on April 30, 2019, the Emperor Shōwa Festival continues as the most recent commemoration of a deceased emperor into the [[:en:Reiwa era|Reiwa period]].
On the day of Senteisai, it was previously designated a public holiday under the former laws "Holiday Law" and "Regulations on Holidays." However, it was removed from the list of holidays with the enactment of the current "Act on National Holidays" in 1948.
{| class="wikitable" |+ Senteisai after the Meiji period |- ! Era !! Senteisai !! Date |- | [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] || [[:en:Emperor Kōmei|Emperor Kōmei]] Festival || January 30 |- | [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] || [[:en:Emperor Meiji|Emperor Meiji]] Festival || July 30 |- | [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] || [[:en:Emperor Taishō|Emperor Taishō]] Festival || December 25 |- | [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] || Emperor Shōwa Festival || January 7 |- | [[:en:Reiwa era|Reiwa]] || Emperor Shōwa Festival || January 7 |}
=== Akama Shrine (Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi Prefecture) Festival === Following the [[:en:Battle of Dan-no-ura|Battle of Dan-no-ura]] in 1186 (Bunji 2), [[:en:Emperor Go-Toba|Emperor Go-Toba]] issued a decree to Nagato Province for the enshrinement of Emperor Antoku's remains at Amidaji Temple (now Akama Shrine). A commemorative festival called the "Senteikai" was held annually on his death anniversary (March 24), which later became the "Main Festival" on April 23 under the Gregorian calendar and was eventually moved to the current date of May 2.
On May 2, ceremonies such as the "Goryōmae Festival," attended by the {{ill|National Heike Association|ja|全国平家会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Heike Association|1=National Heike Association|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11389374}}, are held. On May 3, the "Nakajima Group's Pilgrimage Ceremony" follows, inspired by a story of loyalists visiting the shrine. This is succeeded by the "U11er-class Women's Procession" (Jōrō Dōchū), a tradition rooted in the courtesans and later geisha who honored Emperor Antoku. The procession features participants dressed in court attire, ste11ing in the traditional {{ill|Soto Hachimonji|ja|外八文字|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soto Hachimonji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} style through Shimonoseki.
The main festival concludes on May 4 with the [[:en:Shinko-shiki|Shinko Festival]].
This festival, alongside the {{ill|Kokura Gion Festival|ja|小倉祇園祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokura Gion Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} across the Kanmon Straits in Kitakyushu, is known for the saying, "If it doesn't rain at Senteisai in Seki or Gion in Kokura, money will pour like rain," highlighting the lively crowds.
The festival is designated an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 民俗文化財|1=Folk Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24895900}} of Shimonoseki City<ref>[http://www.stca-kanko.or.jp/sightseeing/city_summary/events/ Shimonoseki Tourism and Convention Association]</ref>.

This festival, alongside the {{ill|Kokura Gion Festival|ja|小倉祇園祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokura Gion Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} across the Kanmon Straits in Kitakyushu, is known for the saying, "If it doesn't rain at Senteisai in Seki or Gion in Kokura, money will pour like rain," highlighting the lively crowds.
The festival is designated an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 民俗文化財|1=Folk Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24895900}} of Shimonoseki City<ref>[http://www.stca-kanko.or.jp/sightseeing/city_summary/events/ Shimonoseki Tourism and Convention Association]</ref>.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:先帝祭]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11388139]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:53, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:下関豊前田商店街_先帝祭_上臈道中_壱.jpg|thumb| The Late Emperor's Festival: Procession of Ladies (Shimonoseki Toyomaeda Sho11ing Arcade)]] [[File:下関豊前田商店街_先帝祭_上臈道中_五.jpg|thumb| The Late Emperor's Festival: Procession of Ladies (Shimonoseki Toyomaeda Sho11ing Arcade)]] {{User:Immanuelle/Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace updated}} '''Senteisai''' (先帝祭) is a festival commemorating the previous emperor.
== Classifications == Examples include: # [[:en:Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace|Ceremonies of the Japanese Imperial Palace]] honoring the previous [[:en:Emperor of Japan|Emperor of Japan]]. These major festivals are held annually on the day of the late emperor's passing. # A festival held at [[:en:Akama Shrine|Akama Shrine]] in [[:en:Shimonoseki|Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi]], Yamaguchi Prefecture, from May 2 to May 4. In this case, the "previous emperor" specifically refers to [[:en:Emperor Antoku|Emperor Antoku]].
=== Imperial Court Rites === As one of the major rites defined by the {{ill|Imperial Household Rites Act|ja|皇室祭祀令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Rites Ordinance|qq=}}, it is held annually on the day of the previous emperor's passing. The actual name of the ritual replaces "Senteisai" with the posthumous title of the emperor.
Although the Imperial Household Rites Ordinance was abolished in 1947, the tradition continues as an Imperial Court rite. During the [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei period]], the {{ill|Emperor Shōwa Festival|ja|昭和天皇祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Shōwa Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was conducted, and following the abdication of Emperor Akihito on April 30, 2019, the Emperor Shōwa Festival continues as the most recent commemoration of a deceased emperor into the [[:en:Reiwa era|Reiwa period]].
On the day of Senteisai, it was previously designated a public holiday under the former laws "Holiday Law" and "Regulations on Holidays." However, it was removed from the list of holidays with the enactment of the current "Act on National Holidays" in 1948.
{| class="wikitable" |+ Senteisai after the Meiji period |- ! Era !! Senteisai !! Date |- | [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] || [[:en:Emperor Kōmei|Emperor Kōmei]] Festival || January 30 |- | [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] || [[:en:Emperor Meiji|Emperor Meiji]] Festival || July 30 |- | [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] || [[:en:Emperor Taishō|Emperor Taishō]] Festival || December 25 |- | [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] || Emperor Shōwa Festival || January 7 |- | [[:en:Reiwa era|Reiwa]] || Emperor Shōwa Festival || January 7 |}
=== Akama Shrine (Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi Prefecture) Festival === Following the [[:en:Battle of Dan-no-ura|Battle of Dan-no-ura]] in 1186 (Bunji 2), [[:en:Emperor Go-Toba|Emperor Go-Toba]] issued a decree to Nagato Province for the enshrinement of Emperor Antoku's remains at Amidaji Temple (now Akama Shrine). A commemorative festival called the "Senteikai" was held annually on his death anniversary (March 24), which later became the "Main Festival" on April 23 under the Gregorian calendar and was eventually moved to the current date of May 2.
On May 2, ceremonies such as the "Goryōmae Festival," attended by the {{ill|National Heike Association|ja|全国平家会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Heike Association|1=National Heike Association|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11389374}}, are held. On May 3, the "Nakajima Group's Pilgrimage Ceremony" follows, inspired by a story of loyalists visiting the shrine. This is succeeded by the "U11er-class Women's Procession" (Jōrō Dōchū), a tradition rooted in the courtesans and later geisha who honored Emperor Antoku. The procession features participants dressed in court attire, ste11ing in the traditional {{ill|Soto Hachimonji|ja|外八文字|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soto Hachimonji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} style through Shimonoseki.
The main festival concludes on May 4 with the [[:en:Shinko-shiki|Shinko Festival]].
This festival, alongside the {{ill|Kokura Gion Festival|ja|小倉祇園祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokura Gion Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} across the Kanmon Straits in Kitakyushu, is known for the saying, "If it doesn't rain at Senteisai in Seki or Gion in Kokura, money will pour like rain," highlighting the lively crowds.
The festival is designated an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 民俗文化財|1=Folk Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24895900}} of Shimonoseki City<ref>[http://www.stca-kanko.or.jp/sightseeing/city_summary/events/ Shimonoseki Tourism and Convention Association]</ref>.
== References == * 'Shinto Encyclopedia' (Heibonsha, reprinted by Rinsen Shoten, 1969), ISBN 4-653-01347-0.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Kokuki|ja|国忌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuki|1=Kokuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11420927}} * {{ill|Shimonoseki red-light district|ja|下関の遊廓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimonoseki red-light district|1=yūkaku in Shimonoseki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28683527}}

On the day of Senteisai, it was previously designated a public holiday under the former laws "Holiday Law" and "Regulations on Holidays." However, it was removed from the list of holidays with the enactment of the current "Act on National Holidays" in 1948.

During the [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei period]], the {{ill|Emperor Shōwa Festival|ja|昭和天皇祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Shōwa Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was conducted, and following the abdication of Emperor Akihito on April 30, 2019, the Emperor Shōwa Festival continues as the most recent commemoration of a deceased emperor into the [[:en:Reiwa era|Reiwa period]].
On the day of Senteisai, it was previously designated a public holiday under the former laws "Holiday Law" and "Regulations on Holidays." However, it was removed from the list of holidays with the enactment of the current "Act on National Holidays" in 1948.

=== Akama Shrine (Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi Prefecture) Festival === Following the [[:en:Battle of Dan-no-ura|Battle of Dan-no-ura]] in 1186 (Bunji 2), [[:en:Emperor Go-Toba|Emperor Go-Toba]] issued a decree to Nagato Province for the enshrinement of Emperor Antoku's remains at Amidaji Temple (now Akama Shrine). A commemorative festival called the "Senteikai" was held annually on his death anniversary (March 24), which later became the "Main Festival" on April 23 under the Gregorian calendar and was eventually moved to the current date of May 2.
On May 2, ceremonies such as the "Goryōmae Festival," attended by the {{ill|National Heike Association|ja|全国平家会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Heike Association|1=National Heike Association|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11389374}}, are held. On May 3, the "Nakajima Group's Pilgrimage Ceremony" follows, inspired by a story of loyalists visiting the shrine. This is succeeded by the "U11er-class Women's Procession" (Jōrō Dōchū), a tradition rooted in the courtesans and later geisha who honored Emperor Antoku. The procession features participants dressed in court attire, ste11ing in the traditional {{ill|Soto Hachimonji|ja|外八文字|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soto Hachimonji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} style through Shimonoseki.
The main festival concludes on May 4 with the [[:en:Shinko-shiki|Shinko Festival]].
This festival, alongside the {{ill|Kokura Gion Festival|ja|小倉祇園祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokura Gion Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} across the Kanmon Straits in Kitakyushu, is known for the saying, "If it doesn't rain at Senteisai in Seki or Gion in Kokura, money will pour like rain," highlighting the lively crowds.
The festival is designated an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 民俗文化財|1=Folk Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24895900}} of Shimonoseki City<ref>[http://www.stca-kanko.or.jp/sightseeing/city_summary/events/ Shimonoseki Tourism and Convention Association]</ref>.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:先帝祭]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:先帝祭]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:先帝祭]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11388139]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:53, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:下関豊前田商店街_先帝祭_上臈道中_壱.jpg|thumb| The Late Emperor's Festival: Procession of Ladies (Shimonoseki Toyomaeda Sho11ing Arcade)]] [[File:下関豊前田商店街_先帝祭_上臈道中_五.jpg|thumb| The Late Emperor's Festival: Procession of Ladies (Shimonoseki Toyomaeda Sho11ing Arcade)]] {{User:Immanuelle/Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace updated}} '''Senteisai''' (先帝祭) is a festival commemorating the previous emperor.
== Classifications == Examples include: # [[:en:Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace|Ceremonies of the Japanese Imperial Palace]] honoring the previous [[:en:Emperor of Japan|Emperor of Japan]]. These major festivals are held annually on the day of the late emperor's passing. # A festival held at [[:en:Akama Shrine|Akama Shrine]] in [[:en:Shimonoseki|Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi]], Yamaguchi Prefecture, from May 2 to May 4. In this case, the "previous emperor" specifically refers to [[:en:Emperor Antoku|Emperor Antoku]].
=== Imperial Court Rites === As one of the major rites defined by the {{ill|Imperial Household Rites Act|ja|皇室祭祀令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Rites Ordinance|qq=}}, it is held annually on the day of the previous emperor's passing. The actual name of the ritual replaces "Senteisai" with the posthumous title of the emperor.
Although the Imperial Household Rites Ordinance was abolished in 1947, the tradition continues as an Imperial Court rite. During the [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei period]], the {{ill|Emperor Shōwa Festival|ja|昭和天皇祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Shōwa Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was conducted, and following the abdication of Emperor Akihito on April 30, 2019, the Emperor Shōwa Festival continues as the most recent commemoration of a deceased emperor into the [[:en:Reiwa era|Reiwa period]].
On the day of Senteisai, it was previously designated a public holiday under the former laws "Holiday Law" and "Regulations on Holidays." However, it was removed from the list of holidays with the enactment of the current "Act on National Holidays" in 1948.
{| class="wikitable" |+ Senteisai after the Meiji period |- ! Era !! Senteisai !! Date |- | [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] || [[:en:Emperor Kōmei|Emperor Kōmei]] Festival || January 30 |- | [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] || [[:en:Emperor Meiji|Emperor Meiji]] Festival || July 30 |- | [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] || [[:en:Emperor Taishō|Emperor Taishō]] Festival || December 25 |- | [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] || Emperor Shōwa Festival || January 7 |- | [[:en:Reiwa era|Reiwa]] || Emperor Shōwa Festival || January 7 |}
=== Akama Shrine (Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi Prefecture) Festival === Following the [[:en:Battle of Dan-no-ura|Battle of Dan-no-ura]] in 1186 (Bunji 2), [[:en:Emperor Go-Toba|Emperor Go-Toba]] issued a decree to Nagato Province for the enshrinement of Emperor Antoku's remains at Amidaji Temple (now Akama Shrine). A commemorative festival called the "Senteikai" was held annually on his death anniversary (March 24), which later became the "Main Festival" on April 23 under the Gregorian calendar and was eventually moved to the current date of May 2.
On May 2, ceremonies such as the "Goryōmae Festival," attended by the {{ill|National Heike Association|ja|全国平家会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Heike Association|1=National Heike Association|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11389374}}, are held. On May 3, the "Nakajima Group's Pilgrimage Ceremony" follows, inspired by a story of loyalists visiting the shrine. This is succeeded by the "U11er-class Women's Procession" (Jōrō Dōchū), a tradition rooted in the courtesans and later geisha who honored Emperor Antoku. The procession features participants dressed in court attire, ste11ing in the traditional {{ill|Soto Hachimonji|ja|外八文字|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soto Hachimonji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} style through Shimonoseki.
The main festival concludes on May 4 with the [[:en:Shinko-shiki|Shinko Festival]].
This festival, alongside the {{ill|Kokura Gion Festival|ja|小倉祇園祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokura Gion Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} across the Kanmon Straits in Kitakyushu, is known for the saying, "If it doesn't rain at Senteisai in Seki or Gion in Kokura, money will pour like rain," highlighting the lively crowds.
The festival is designated an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 民俗文化財|1=Folk Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24895900}} of Shimonoseki City<ref>[http://www.stca-kanko.or.jp/sightseeing/city_summary/events/ Shimonoseki Tourism and Convention Association]</ref>.
== References == * 'Shinto Encyclopedia' (Heibonsha, reprinted by Rinsen Shoten, 1969), ISBN 4-653-01347-0.

The procession features participants dressed in court attire, ste11ing in the traditional {{ill|Soto Hachimonji|ja|外八文字|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soto Hachimonji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} style through Shimonoseki.
The main festival concludes on May 4 with the {{ill|Shinko-shiki|en|Shinko-shiki|WD=Q123907414|lt=Shinko Festival}}.
This festival, alongside the {{ill|Kokura Gion Festival|ja|小倉祇園祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokura Gion Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} across the Kanmon Straits in Kitakyushu, is known for the saying, "If it doesn't rain at Senteisai in Seki or Gion in Kokura, money will pour like rain," highlighting the lively crowds.
The festival is designated an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 民俗文化財|1=Folk Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24895900}} of Shimonoseki City<ref>[http://www.stca-kanko.or.jp/sightseeing/city_summary/events/ Shimonoseki Tourism and Convention Association]</ref>.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:先帝祭]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:先帝祭]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:先帝祭]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11388139]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:53, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:下関豊前田商店街_先帝祭_上臈道中_壱.jpg|thumb| The Late Emperor's Festival: Procession of Ladies (Shimonoseki Toyomaeda Sho11ing Arcade)]] [[File:下関豊前田商店街_先帝祭_上臈道中_五.jpg|thumb| The Late Emperor's Festival: Procession of Ladies (Shimonoseki Toyomaeda Sho11ing Arcade)]] {{User:Immanuelle/Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace updated}} '''Senteisai''' (先帝祭) is a festival commemorating the previous emperor.
== Classifications == Examples include: # {{ill|Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace|en|Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace|WD=Q11453226|lt=Ceremonies of the Japanese Imperial Palace}} honoring the previous {{ill|Emperor of Japan|en|Emperor of Japan|WD=Q208233|lt=Emperor of Japan}}. These major festivals are held annually on the day of the late emperor's passing. # A festival held at {{ill|Akama Shrine|en|Akama Shrine|WD=Q712617|lt=Akama Shrine}} in {{ill|Shimonoseki|en|Shimonoseki|WD=Q203308|lt=Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi}}, Yamaguchi Prefecture, from May 2 to May 4. In this case, the "previous emperor" specifically refers to {{ill|Emperor Antoku|en|Emperor Antoku|WD=Q316651|lt=Emperor Antoku}}.
=== Imperial Court Rites === As one of the major rites defined by the {{ill|Imperial Household Rites Act|ja|皇室祭祀令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Rites Ordinance|qq=}}, it is held annually on the day of the previous emperor's passing. The actual name of the ritual replaces "Senteisai" with the posthumous title of the emperor.
Although the Imperial Household Rites Ordinance was abolished in 1947, the tradition continues as an Imperial Court rite. During the {{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|WD=Q234510|lt=Heisei period}}, the {{ill|Emperor Shōwa Festival|ja|昭和天皇祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Shōwa Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was conducted, and following the abdication of Emperor Akihito on April 30, 2019, the Emperor Shōwa Festival continues as the most recent commemoration of a deceased emperor into the {{ill|Reiwa era|en|Reiwa era|WD=Q62609017|lt=Reiwa period}}.
On the day of Senteisai, it was previously designated a public holiday under the former laws "Holiday Law" and "Regulations on Holidays." However, it was removed from the list of holidays with the enactment of the current "Act on National Holidays" in 1948.
{| class="wikitable" |+ Senteisai after the Meiji period |- ! Era !! Senteisai !! Date |- | {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} || {{ill|Emperor Kōmei|en|Emperor Kōmei|WD=Q299179|lt=Emperor Kōmei}} Festival || January 30 |- | {{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|WD=Q329174|lt=Taishō}} || {{ill|Emperor Meiji|en|Emperor Meiji|WD=Q174231|lt=Emperor Meiji}} Festival || July 30 |- | {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Shōwa}} || {{ill|Emperor Taishō|en|Emperor Taishō|WD=Q157052|lt=Emperor Taishō}} Festival || December 25 |- | {{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|WD=Q234510|lt=Heisei}} || Emperor Shōwa Festival || January 7 |- | {{ill|Reiwa era|en|Reiwa era|WD=Q62609017|lt=Reiwa}} || Emperor Shōwa Festival || January 7 |}
=== Akama Shrine (Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi Prefecture) Festival === Following the {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|en|Battle of Dan-no-ura|WD=Q968676|lt=Battle of Dan-no-ura}} in 1186 (Bunji 2), {{ill|Emperor Go-Toba|en|Emperor Go-Toba|WD=Q316658|lt=Emperor Go-Toba}} issued a decree to Nagato Province for the enshrinement of Emperor Antoku's remains at Amidaji Temple (now Akama Shrine). A commemorative festival called the "Senteikai" was held annually on his death anniversary (March 24), which later became the "Main Festival" on April 23 under the Gregorian calendar and was eventually moved to the current date of May 2.
On May 2, ceremonies such as the "Goryōmae Festival," attended by the {{ill|National Heike Association|ja|全国平家会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Heike Association|1=National Heike Association|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11389374}}, are held. On May 3, the "Nakajima Group's Pilgrimage Ceremony" follows, inspired by a story of loyalists visiting the shrine. This is succeeded by the "U11er-class Women's Procession" (Jōrō Dōchū), a tradition rooted in the courtesans and later geisha who honored Emperor Antoku. The procession features participants dressed in court attire, ste11ing in the traditional {{ill|Soto Hachimonji|ja|外八文字|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soto Hachimonji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} style through Shimonoseki.
The main festival concludes on May 4 with the {{ill|Shinko-shiki|en|Shinko-shiki|WD=Q123907414|lt=Shinko Festival}}.
This festival, alongside the {{ill|Kokura Gion Festival|ja|小倉祇園祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokura Gion Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} across the Kanmon Straits in Kitakyushu, is known for the saying, "If it doesn't rain at Senteisai in Seki or Gion in Kokura, money will pour like rain," highlighting the lively crowds.
The festival is designated an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 民俗文化財|1=Folk Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24895900}} of Shimonoseki City<ref>[http://www.stca-kanko.or.jp/sightseeing/city_summary/events/ Shimonoseki Tourism and Convention Association]</ref>.
== References == * 'Shinto Encyclopedia' (Heibonsha, reprinted by Rinsen Shoten, 1969), ISBN 4-653-01347-0.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Kokuki|ja|国忌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuki|1=Kokuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11420927}} * {{ill|Shimonoseki red-light district|ja|下関の遊廓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimonoseki red-light district|1=yūkaku in Shimonoseki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28683527}}
== External Links == * [http://www.tiki.ne.jp/~akama-jingu/senteisai.html Senteisai] (Akama Shrine Official Website)

[[File:下関豊前田商店街_先帝祭_上臈道中_壱.jpg|thumb| The Late Emperor's Festival: Procession of Ladies (Shimonoseki Toyomaeda Sho11ing Arcade)]] [[File:下関豊前田商店街_先帝祭_上臈道中_五.jpg|thumb| The Late Emperor's Festival: Procession of Ladies (Shimonoseki Toyomaeda Sho11ing Arcade)]] {{User:Immanuelle/Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace updated}} '''Senteisai''' (先帝祭) is a festival commemorating the previous emperor.
== Classifications == Examples include: # {{ill|Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace|en|Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace|WD=Q11453226|lt=Ceremonies of the Japanese Imperial Palace}} honoring the previous {{ill|Emperor of Japan|en|Emperor of Japan|WD=Q208233|lt=Emperor of Japan}}. These major festivals are held annually on the day of the late emperor's passing. # A festival held at {{ill|Akama Shrine|en|Akama Shrine|WD=Q712617|lt=Akama Shrine}} in {{ill|Shimonoseki|en|Shimonoseki|WD=Q203308|lt=Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi}}, Yamaguchi Prefecture, from May 2 to May 4. In this case, the "previous emperor" specifically refers to {{ill|Emperor Antoku|en|Emperor Antoku|WD=Q316651|lt=Emperor Antoku}}.
=== Imperial Court Rites === As one of the major rites defined by the {{ill|Imperial Household Rites Act|ja|皇室祭祀令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Rites Ordinance|qq=}}, it is held annually on the day of the previous emperor's passing. The actual name of the ritual replaces "Senteisai" with the posthumous title of the emperor.
Although the Imperial Household Rites Ordinance was abolished in 1947, the tradition continues as an Imperial Court rite. During the {{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|WD=Q234510|lt=Heisei period}}, the {{ill|Emperor Shōwa Festival|ja|昭和天皇祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Shōwa Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was conducted, and following the abdication of Emperor Akihito on April 30, 2019, the Emperor Shōwa Festival continues as the most recent commemoration of a deceased emperor into the {{ill|Reiwa era|en|Reiwa era|WD=Q62609017|lt=Reiwa period}}.
On the day of Senteisai, it was previously designated a public holiday under the former laws "Holiday Law" and "Regulations on Holidays." However, it was removed from the list of holidays with the enactment of the current "Act on National Holidays" in 1948.
{| class="wikitable" |+ Senteisai after the Meiji period |- ! Era !! Senteisai !! Date |- | {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} || {{ill|Emperor Kōmei|en|Emperor Kōmei|WD=Q299179|lt=Emperor Kōmei}} Festival || January 30 |- | {{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|WD=Q329174|lt=Taishō}} || {{ill|Emperor Meiji|en|Emperor Meiji|WD=Q174231|lt=Emperor Meiji}} Festival || July 30 |- | {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Shōwa}} || {{ill|Emperor Taishō|en|Emperor Taishō|WD=Q157052|lt=Emperor Taishō}} Festival || December 25 |- | {{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|WD=Q234510|lt=Heisei}} || Emperor Shōwa Festival || January 7 |- | {{ill|Reiwa era|en|Reiwa era|WD=Q62609017|lt=Reiwa}} || Emperor Shōwa Festival || January 7 |}
=== Akama Shrine (Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi Prefecture) Festival === Following the {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|en|Battle of Dan-no-ura|WD=Q968676|lt=Battle of Dan-no-ura}} in 1186 (Bunji 2), {{ill|Emperor Go-Toba|en|Emperor Go-Toba|WD=Q316658|lt=Emperor Go-Toba}} issued a decree to Nagato Province for the enshrinement of Emperor Antoku's remains at Amidaji Temple (now Akama Shrine). A commemorative festival called the "Senteikai" was held annually on his death anniversary (March 24), which later became the "Main Festival" on April 23 under the Gregorian calendar and was eventually moved to the current date of May 2.
On May 2, ceremonies such as the "Goryōmae Festival," attended by the {{ill|National Heike Association|ja|全国平家会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Heike Association|1=National Heike Association|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11389374}}, are held. On May 3, the "Nakajima Group's Pilgrimage Ceremony" follows, inspired by a story of loyalists visiting the shrine. This is succeeded by the "U11er-class Women's Procession" (Jōrō Dōchū), a tradition rooted in the courtesans and later geisha who honored Emperor Antoku. The procession features participants dressed in court attire, ste11ing in the traditional {{ill|Soto Hachimonji|ja|外八文字|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soto Hachimonji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} style through Shimonoseki.
The main festival concludes on May 4 with the {{ill|Shinko-shiki|en|Shinko-shiki|WD=Q123907414|lt=Shinko Festival}}.
This festival, alongside the {{ill|Kokura Gion Festival|ja|小倉祇園祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokura Gion Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} across the Kanmon Straits in Kitakyushu, is known for the saying, "If it doesn't rain at Senteisai in Seki or Gion in Kokura, money will pour like rain," highlighting the lively crowds.
The festival is designated an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 民俗文化財|1=Folk Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24895900}} of Shimonoseki City<ref>[http://www.stca-kanko.or.jp/sightseeing/city_summary/events/ Shimonoseki Tourism and Convention Association]</ref>.
== References == * 'Shinto Encyclopedia' (Heibonsha, reprinted by Rinsen Shoten, 1969), ISBN 4-653-01347-0.

== External Links == * [http://www.tiki.ne.jp/~akama-jingu/senteisai.html Senteisai] (Akama Shrine Official Website)

=== Akama Shrine (Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi Prefecture) Festival === Following the {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|en|Battle of Dan-no-ura|WD=Q968676|lt=Battle of Dan-no-ura}} in 1186 (Bunji 2), {{ill|Emperor Go-Toba|en|Emperor Go-Toba|WD=Q316658|lt=Emperor Go-Toba}} issued a decree to Nagato Province for the enshrinement of Emperor Antoku's remains at Amidaji Temple (now Akama Shrine). Date |- | {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} || {{ill|Emperor Kōmei|en|Emperor Kōmei|WD=Q299179|lt=Emperor Kōmei|1=Emperor Kōmei}} Festival || January 30 |- | {{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|WD=Q329174|lt=Taishō|1=Taishō era}} || {{ill|Emperor Meiji|en|Emperor Meiji|WD=Q174231|lt=Emperor Meiji|1=Emperor Meiji}} Festival || July 30 |- | {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa era}} || {{ill|Emperor Taishō|en|Emperor Taishō|WD=Q157052|lt=Emperor Taishō|1=Emperor Taishō}} Festival || December 25 |- | {{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|WD=Q234510|lt=Heisei|1=Heisei era}} || Emperor Shōwa Festival || January 7 |- | {{ill|Reiwa era|en|Reiwa era|WD=Q62609017|lt=Reiwa|1=Reiwa era}} || Emperor Shōwa Festival || January 7 |}
=== Akama Shrine (Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi Prefecture) Festival === Following the {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|en|Battle of Dan-no-ura|WD=Q968676|lt=Battle of Dan-no-ura|1=Battle of Dan-no-ura}} in 1186 (Bunji 2), {{ill|Emperor Go-Toba|en|Emperor Go-Toba|WD=Q316658|lt=Emperor Go-Toba|1=Emperor Go-Toba}} issued a decree to Nagato Province for the enshrinement of Emperor Antoku's remains at Amidaji Temple (now Akama Shrine). A commemorative festival called the "Senteikai" was held annually on his death anniversary (March 24), which later became the "Main Festival" on April 23 under the Gregorian calendar and was eventually moved to the current date of May 2.
On May 2, ceremonies such as the "Goryōmae Festival," attended by the {{ill|National Heike Association|ja|全国平家会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Heike Association|1=National Heike Association|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11389374}}, are held. On May 3, the "Nakajima Group's Pilgrimage Ceremony" follows, inspired by a story of loyalists visiting the shrine. This is succeeded by the "U11er-class Women's Procession" (Jōrō Dōchū), a tradition rooted in the courtesans and later geisha who honored Emperor Antoku. The procession features participants dressed in court attire, ste11ing in the traditional {{ill|Soto Hachimonji|ja|外八文字|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soto Hachimonji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} style through Shimonoseki.
The main festival concludes on May 4 with the {{ill|Shinko-shiki|en|Shinko-shiki|WD=Q123907414|lt=Shinko Festival|1=Shinko-shiki}}.
This festival, alongside the {{ill|Kokura Gion Festival|ja|小倉祇園祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokura Gion Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} across the Kanmon Straits in Kitakyushu, is known for the saying, "If it doesn't rain at Senteisai in Seki or Gion in Kokura, money will pour like rain," highlighting the lively crowds.
The festival is designated an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 民俗文化財|1=Folk Cultural Property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24895900}} of Shimonoseki City<ref>[http://www.stca-kanko.or.jp/sightseeing/city_summary/events/ Shimonoseki Tourism and Convention Association]</ref>.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:先帝祭]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:先帝祭]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:先帝祭]]
[[File:下関豊前田商店街_先帝祭_上臈道中_壱.jpg|thumb| The Late Emperor's Festival: Procession of Ladies (Shimonoseki Toyomaeda Sho11ing Arcade)]] [[File:下関豊前田商店街_先帝祭_上臈道中_五.jpg|thumb| The Late Emperor's Festival: Procession of Ladies (Shimonoseki Toyomaeda Sho11ing Arcade)]] {{User:Immanuelle/Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace updated}} '''Senteisai''' (先帝祭) is a festival commemorating the previous emperor.
== Classifications == Examples include: # {{ill|Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace|en|Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace|WD=Q11453226|lt=Ceremonies of the Japanese Imperial Palace|1=Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace}} honoring the previous {{ill|Emperor of Japan|en|Emperor of Japan|WD=Q208233|lt=Emperor of Japan|1=Emperor of Japan}}. These major festivals are held annually on the day of the late emperor's passing. # A festival held at {{ill|Akama Shrine|en|Akama Shrine|WD=Q712617|lt=Akama Shrine|1=Akama Shrine}} in {{ill|Shimonoseki|en|Shimonoseki|WD=Q203308|lt=Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi|1=Shimonoseki}}, Yamaguchi Prefecture, from May 2 to May 4. In this case, the "previous emperor" specifically refers to {{ill|Emperor Antoku|en|Emperor Antoku|WD=Q316651|lt=Emperor Antoku|1=Emperor Antoku}}.
=== Imperial Court Rites === As one of the major rites defined by the {{ill|Imperial Household Rites Act|ja|皇室祭祀令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Rites Ordinance|qq=}}, it is held annually on the day of the previous emperor's passing. The actual name of the ritual replaces "Senteisai" with the posthumous title of the emperor.
Although the Imperial Household Rites Ordinance was abolished in 1947, the tradition continues as an Imperial Court rite. During the {{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|WD=Q234510|lt=Heisei period|1=Heisei era}}, the {{ill|Emperor Shōwa Festival|ja|昭和天皇祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Shōwa Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was conducted, and following the abdication of Emperor Akihito on April 30, 2019, the Emperor Shōwa Festival continues as the most recent commemoration of a deceased emperor into the {{ill|Reiwa era|en|Reiwa era|WD=Q62609017|lt=Reiwa period|1=Reiwa era}}.
On the day of Senteisai, it was previously designated a public holiday under the former laws "Holiday Law" and "Regulations on Holidays." However, it was removed from the list of holidays with the enactment of the current "Act on National Holidays" in 1948.

On May 3, the "Nakajima Group's Pilgrimage Ceremony" follows, inspired by a story of loyalists visiting the shrine. This is succeeded by the "U11er-class Women's Procession" (Jōrō Dōchū), a tradition rooted in the courtesans and later geisha who honored Emperor Antoku. The procession features participants dressed in court attire, ste11ing in the traditional {{ill|Soto Hachimonji|ja|外八文字|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soto Hachimonji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} style through Shimonoseki.
The main festival concludes on May 4 with the {{ill|Shinko-shiki|en|Shinko-shiki|WD=Q123907414|lt=Shinko Festival|1=Shinko-shiki}}.
This festival, alongside the {{ill|Kokura Gion Festival|ja|小倉祇園祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokura Gion Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} across the Kanmon Straits in Kitakyushu, is known for the saying, "If it doesn't rain at Senteisai in Seki or Gion in Kokura, money will pour like rain," highlighting the lively crowds.
The festival is designated an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 民俗文化財|1=Folk Cultural Property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24895900}} of Shimonoseki City<ref>[http://www.stca-kanko.or.jp/sightseeing/city_summary/events/ Shimonoseki Tourism and Convention Association]</ref>.
== References == * 'Shinto Encyclopedia' (Heibonsha, reprinted by Rinsen Shoten, 1969), ISBN 4-653-01347-0.

revision.text:
[[File:下関豊前田商店街_先帝祭_上臈道中_壱.jpg|thumb| The Late Emperor's Festival: Procession of Ladies (Shimonoseki Toyomaeda Sho11ing Arcade)]] [[File:下関豊前田商店街_先帝祭_上臈道中_五.jpg|thumb| The Late Emperor's Festival: Procession of Ladies (Shimonoseki Toyomaeda Sho11ing Arcade)]] {{User:Immanuelle/Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace updated}} '''Senteisai''' (先帝祭) is a festival commemorating the previous emperor.
== Classifications == Examples include: # {{ill|Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace|en|Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace|WD=Q11453226|lt=Ceremonies of the Japanese Imperial Palace|1=Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace}} honoring the previous {{ill|Emperor of Japan|en|Emperor of Japan|WD=Q208233|lt=Emperor of Japan|1=Emperor of Japan}}. These major festivals are held annually on the day of the late emperor's passing. # A festival held at {{ill|Akama Shrine|en|Akama Shrine|WD=Q712617|lt=Akama Shrine|1=Akama Shrine}} in {{ill|Shimonoseki|en|Shimonoseki|WD=Q203308|lt=Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi|1=Shimonoseki}}, Yamaguchi Prefecture, from May 2 to May 4. In this case, the "previous emperor" specifically refers to {{ill|Emperor Antoku|en|Emperor Antoku|WD=Q316651|lt=Emperor Antoku|1=Emperor Antoku}}.
=== Imperial Court Rites === As one of the major rites defined by the {{ill|Imperial Household Rites Act|ja|皇室祭祀令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Rites Ordinance|qq=}}, it is held annually on the day of the previous emperor's passing. The actual name of the ritual replaces "Senteisai" with the posthumous title of the emperor.
Although the Imperial Household Rites Ordinance was abolished in 1947, the tradition continues as an Imperial Court rite. During the {{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|WD=Q234510|lt=Heisei period|1=Heisei era}}, the {{ill|Emperor Shōwa Festival|ja|昭和天皇祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Shōwa Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was conducted, and following the abdication of Emperor Akihito on April 30, 2019, the Emperor Shōwa Festival continues as the most recent commemoration of a deceased emperor into the {{ill|Reiwa era|en|Reiwa era|WD=Q62609017|lt=Reiwa period|1=Reiwa era}}.
On the day of Senteisai, it was previously designated a public holiday under the former laws "Holiday Law" and "Regulations on Holidays." However, it was removed from the list of holidays with the enactment of the current "Act on National Holidays" in 1948.
{| class="wikitable" |+ Senteisai after the Meiji period |- ! Era !! Senteisai !! Date |- | {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} || {{ill|Emperor Kōmei|en|Emperor Kōmei|WD=Q299179|lt=Emperor Kōmei|1=Emperor Kōmei}} Festival || January 30 |- | {{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|WD=Q329174|lt=Taishō|1=Taishō era}} || {{ill|Emperor Meiji|en|Emperor Meiji|WD=Q174231|lt=Emperor Meiji|1=Emperor Meiji}} Festival || July 30 |- | {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa era}} || {{ill|Emperor Taishō|en|Emperor Taishō|WD=Q157052|lt=Emperor Taishō|1=Emperor Taishō}} Festival || December 25 |- | {{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|WD=Q234510|lt=Heisei|1=Heisei era}} || Emperor Shōwa Festival || January 7 |- | {{ill|Reiwa era|en|Reiwa era|WD=Q62609017|lt=Reiwa|1=Reiwa era}} || Emperor Shōwa Festival || January 7 |}
=== Akama Shrine (Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi Prefecture) Festival === Following the {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|en|Battle of Dan-no-ura|WD=Q968676|lt=Battle of Dan-no-ura|1=Battle of Dan-no-ura}} in 1186 (Bunji 2), {{ill|Emperor Go-Toba|en|Emperor Go-Toba|WD=Q316658|lt=Emperor Go-Toba|1=Emperor Go-Toba}} issued a decree to Nagato Province for the enshrinement of Emperor Antoku's remains at Amidaji Temple (now Akama Shrine). A commemorative festival called the "Senteikai" was held annually on his death anniversary (March 24), which later became the "Main Festival" on April 23 under the Gregorian calendar and was eventually moved to the current date of May 2.
On May 2, ceremonies such as the "Goryōmae Festival," attended by the {{ill|National Heike Association|ja|全国平家会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Heike Association|1=National Heike Association|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11389374}}, are held. On May 3, the "Nakajima Group's Pilgrimage Ceremony" follows, inspired by a story of loyalists visiting the shrine. This is succeeded by the "U11er-class Women's Procession" (Jōrō Dōchū), a tradition rooted in the courtesans and later geisha who honored Emperor Antoku. The procession features participants dressed in court attire, ste11ing in the traditional {{ill|Soto Hachimonji|ja|外八文字|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soto Hachimonji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} style through Shimonoseki.
The main festival concludes on May 4 with the {{ill|Shinko-shiki|en|Shinko-shiki|WD=Q123907414|lt=Shinko Festival|1=Shinko-shiki}}.
This festival, alongside the {{ill|Kokura Gion Festival|ja|小倉祇園祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokura Gion Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} across the Kanmon Straits in Kitakyushu, is known for the saying, "If it doesn't rain at Senteisai in Seki or Gion in Kokura, money will pour like rain," highlighting the lively crowds.
The festival is designated an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 民俗文化財|1=Folk Cultural Property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24895900}} of Shimonoseki City<ref>[http://www.stca-kanko.or.jp/sightseeing/city_summary/events/ Shimonoseki Tourism and Convention Association]</ref>.
